FromRef<S>,\n111\t{\n112\t    let state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n113\t\n114\t    // Parse status filter (supports comma-separated values, takes first)\n115\t    let status_filter =              _ => None,\n125\t            })\n126\t    });\n127\t\n128\t    // Parse indexUids filter (supports comma-separated values, takes first)\n129\t    let index_uid_filter = query.indexUids.as_ref().and_then(|s| {\n130\t        s.split(',')\n131\t            .next()\n132\t            .map(|uid| uid.trim().to_string())\n133\t    });\n134\t\n135\t    // Parse types filter (supports comma-separated values, takes first)\n136\t    let task_type_filter = query.types.as_ref().and_then(|s| {\n137\t        s.split(',')\n138\t            .next()\n139\t            .map(|ty| // List tasks from registry\n153\t    let tasks = state\n154\t        .task_registry\n155\t        .list(filter)\n156\t        .map_err(|_| StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)?;\n157\t\n158\t    // Get total count (without limit/offset)\n159\t    let total = state\n160\t        .task_registry\n161\t        .count();\n162\t\n163\t    // Convert to Meilisearch-compatible response\n164\t    let results = tasks.into_iter().map(task_to_response).collect();\n165\t\n166\t    let limit = query.limit.unwrap_or(20);\n167\t    let from = query.from.unwrap_or(0);\n168\t\n169\t    Ok(Json(TasksListResponse ID.\n178\t///\n179\t/// Polls all mapped nodes for their current task status and aggregates the result.\n180\tasync fn get_task<S>(\n181\t    Path(id): Path<String>,\n182\t    State(state): State<S>,\n183\t) -> Result<Json<TaskResponse>, StatusCode>\n184\twhere\n185\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n186\t    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n187\t{\n188\t    let state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n189\t\n190\t    // Validate task ID format\n191\t    if !id.starts_with(\"mtask-\") {\n192\t        return Err(StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST);\n193\t    }\n194\t\n195\t    let mut task = state\n196\t        .task_registry\n197\t        .get(&id)\n198\t        .map_err(|_| StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)?\n199\t        .ok_or(StatusCode::NOT_FOUND)?;\n200\t\n201\t    // Poll nodes for current status if task is not terminal\n202\t    if !matches!(task.status, TaskStatus::Succeeded | TaskStatus::Failed | TaskStatus::Canceled) {\n203\t        let topology = state.topology.read().await;\n204\t        let client = HttpClient::new(\n205\t            state.config.node_master_key.clone(),\n206\t            state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n207\t        );\n208\t\n209\t        // Update node task statuses by polling each node\n210\t        let mut node_errors = HashMap::new();\n211\t        let mut any_processing = false;\n212\t        let mut all_succeeded = true;\n213\t        let mut any_failed = false;\n214\t\n215\t        for (node_id_str, node_task) in &task.node_tasks {\n216\t            let node_id = miroir_core::topology::NodeId::new(node_id_str.clone());\n217\t\n218\t            // Skip polling if node task is already terminal\n219\t            if matches!(node_task.status, NodeTaskStatus::Succeeded | NodeTaskStatus::Failed) {\n220\t                if matches!(node_task.status, NodeTaskStatus::Failed) {\n221\t                    any_failed = true;\n222\t                    all_succeeded = false;\n223\t                }\n224\t                continue;\n225\t            }\n226\t\n227\t            // Get node address from topology\n228\t            let node = match topology.node(&node_id) {\n229\t                Some(n) => n,\n230\t                None => {\n231\t                    node_errors.insert(node_id_str.clone(), \"node not found in topology\".to_string());\n232\t                    any_failed = true;\n233\t                    all_succeeded = false;\n234\t                    continue;\n235\t                }\n236\t            };\n237\t\n238\t            // Poll this node for task status\n239\t            let req = TaskStatusRequest { task_uid: node_task.task_uid };\n240\t            match client.get_task_status(&node_id, &node.address, &req).await {\n241\t                Ok(resp) => {\n242\t                    let new_status = resp.to_node_status();\n243\t                    // Update the node task status in the registry\n244\t                    let _ = state.task_registry.update_node_task(&id, node_id_str, new_status);\n245\t\n246\t                    // Track overall status\n247\t                    match new_status {\n248\t                        NodeTaskStatus::Succeeded => {}\n249\t                        NodeTaskStatus::Failed => {\n250\t                            any_failed = true;\n251\t                            all_succeeded = false;\n252\t                            if let Some(error) = resp.error {\n253\t                                node_errors.insert(node_id_str.clone(), error);\n254\t                            }\n255\t                        }\n256\t                        NodeTaskStatus::Processing => {\n257\t                            any_processing = true;\n258\t                            all_succeeded = false;\n259\t                        }\n260\t                        NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued => {\n261\t                            all_succeeded = false;\n262\t                        }\n263\t                    }\n264\t                }\n265\t                Err(e) => {\n266\t                    tracing::warn!(node = %node_id_str, task = %id, error = ?e, \"failed to poll node for task\");\n267\t                    // Don't mark as failed on network error - may be transient\n268\t                    all_succeeded = false;\n269\t                }\n270\t            }\n271\t        }\n272\t\n273\t        // Update overall task status based on node task statuses\n274\t        let new_status = if any_failed {\n275\t            TaskStatus::Failed\n276\t        } else if all_succeeded {\n277\t            TaskStatus::Succeeded\n278\t        } else if any_processing {\n279\t            TaskStatus::Processing\n280\t        } else {\n281\t            TaskStatus::Enqueued\n282\t        };\n283\t\n284\t        // Record terminal task status in Prometheus (§10 task metrics)\n285\t        if matches!(new_status, TaskStatus::Succeeded | TaskStatus::Failed | TaskStatus::Canceled) {\n286\t            let status_str = match new_status {\n287\t                TaskStatus::Succeeded => \"succeeded\",\n288\t                TaskStatus::Failed => \"failed\",\n289\t                TaskStatus::Canceled => \"canceled\",\n290\t                _ => unreachable!(),\n291\t            };\n292\t            state.metrics.inc_tasks_total(status_str);\n293\t\n294\t            // Observe task processing age (time from creation to terminal state)\n295\t            let age_ms = {\n296\t                let now_ms = std::time::SystemTime::now()\n297\t                    .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n298\t                    .unwrap_or_default()\n299\t                    .as_millis() as u64;\n300\t                now_ms.saturating_sub(task.created_at)\n301\t            };\n302\t            state.metrics.observe_task_processing_age(age_ms as f64 / 1000.0);\n303\t        }\n304\t\n305\t        // Update the task status in the registry\n306\t        let _ = state.task_registry.update_status(&id, new_status);\n307\t\n308\t        // Update the task with node errors and new status\n309\t        task.status = new_status;\n310\t        task.node_errors = node_errors;\n311\t\n312\t        // Set timestamps\n313\t        if matches!(new_status, TaskStatus::Processing) && task.started_at.is_none() {\n314\t            task.started_at = Some(std::time::SystemTime::now()\n315\t                .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n316\t                .unwrap_or_default()\n317\t                .as_millis() as u64);\n318\t        }\n319\t\n320\t        if matches!(new_status, TaskStatus::Succeeded | TaskStatus::Failed | TaskStatus::Canceled) && task.finished_at.is_none() {\n321\t            task.finished_at = Some(std::time::SystemTime::now()\n322\t                .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n323\t                .unwrap_or_default()\n324\t                .as_millis() as u64);\n325\t        }\n326\t    }\n327\t\n328\t    Ok(Json(task_to_response(task)))\n329\t}\n330\t\n331\t/// DELETE /tasks/{id} — Cancel a task (best-effort).\n332\tasync fn delete_task<S>(\n333\t    Path(id): Path<String>,\n334\t    State(state): State<S>,\n335\t) -> Result<Json<TaskResponse>, StatusCode>\n336\twhere\n337\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n338\t    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n339\t{\n340\t    let state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n341\t\n342\t    // Validate task ID format\n343\t    if !id.starts_with(\"mtask-\") {\n344\t        return Err(StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST);\n345\t    }\n346\t\n347\t    // Get the task first\n348\t    let task = state\n349\t        .task_registry\n350\t        .get(&id)\n351\t        .map_err(|_| StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)?\n352\t        .ok_or(StatusCode::NOT_FOUND)?;\n353\t\n354\t    // Update status to canceled if not already terminal\n355\t    if matches!(task.status, TaskStatus::Enqueued | TaskStatus::Processing) {\n356\t        state\n357\t            .task_registry\n358\t            .update_status(&id, TaskStatus::Canceled)\n359\t            .map_err(|_| StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)?;\n360\t    }\n361\t\n362\t    // Return the updated task\n363\t    let updated = state\n364\t        .task_registry\n365\t        .get(&id)\n366\t        .map_err(|_| StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)?\n367\t        .ok_or(StatusCode::NOT_FOUND)?;\n368\t\n369\t    Ok(Json(task_to_response(updated)))\n370\t}\n371\t\n372\t/// Convert MiroirTask to Meilisearch-compatible TaskResponse.\n373\tfn task_to_response(task: MiroirTask) -> TaskResponse {\n374\t    let status_str = match task.status {\n375\t        TaskStatus::Enqueued => \"enqueued\",\n376\t        TaskStatus::Processing => \"processing\",\n377\t        TaskStatus::Succeeded => \"succeeded\",\n378\t        TaskStatus::Failed => \"failed\",\n379\t        TaskStatus::Canceled => \"canceled\",\n380\t    };\n381\t\n382\t    let enqueued_at = format_millis_timestamp(task.created_at);\n383\t    let started_at = task.started_at.map(|t| format_millis_timestamp(t));\n384\t    let finished_at = task.finished_at.map(|t| format_millis_timestamp(t));\n385\t\n386\t    let error = if task.status == TaskStatus::Failed {\n387\t        Some(TaskError {\n388\t            code: \"internal_error\".to_string(),\n389\t            message: task.error.clone().unwrap_or_else(|| {\n390\t                if task.node_errors.is_empty() {\n391\t                    \"task failed\".to_string()\n392\t                } else {\n393\t                    format!(\"{} node(s) failed\", task.node_errors.len())\n394\t                }\n395\t            }),\n396\t            error_type: \"internal_error\".to_string(),\n397\t            details: task.node_errors.clone(),\n398\t        })\n399\t    } else {\n400\t        None\n401\t    };\n402\t\n403\t    // Build per-node details\n404\t    let mut nodes = HashMap::new();\n405\t    for (node_id, node_task) in &task.node_tasks {\n406\t        let node_status = match node_task.status {\n407\t            miroir_core::task::NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued => \"enqueued\",\n408\t            miroir_core::task::NodeTaskStatus::Processing => \"processing\",\n409\t            miroir_core::task::NodeTaskStatus::Succeeded => \"succeeded\",\n410\t            miroir_core::task::NodeTaskStatus::Failed => \"failed\",\n411\t        };\n412\t        nodes.insert(\n413\t            node_id.clone(),\n414\t            NodeTaskDetail {\n415\t                task_uid: node_task.task_uid,\n416\t                status: node_status.to_string(),\n417\t            },\n418\t        );\n419\t    }\n420\t\n421\t    let details = Some(TaskDetails {\n422\t        received_documents: None,\n423\t        nodes,\n424\t    });\n425\t\n426\t    TaskResponse {\n427\t        task_uid: task.miroir_id,\n428\t        indexUid: task.index_uid,\n429\t        status: status_str.to_string(),\n430\t        task_type: task.task_type,\n431\t        details,\n432\t        error,\n433\t        duration: None,\n434\t        enqueuedAt: enqueued_at,\n435\t        startedAt: started_at,\n436\t        finishedAt: finished_at,\n437\t    }\n438\t}\n439\t\n440\t/// Format milliseconds since epoch as ISO 8601 timestamp.\n441\tfn format_millis_timestamp(millis: u64) -> String {\n442\t    // Simple ISO 8601 format without chrono dependency\n443\t    let secs = millis / 1000;\n444\t    let millis_part = millis % 1000;\n445\t\n446\t    // Calculate date components (simplified, assumes Unix epoch)\n447\t    // This is a rough approximation - for production use chrono or time crate\n448\t    let days_since_epoch = secs / 86400;\n449\t    let seconds_in_day = secs % 86400;\n450\t\n451\t    let hours = seconds_in_day / 3600;\n452\t    let minutes = (seconds_in_day % 3600) / 60;\n453\t    let seconds = seconds_in_day % 60;\n454\t\n455\t    // Days from 1970-01-01 to 2000-01-01 is roughly 10957 days\n456\t    // This is a very rough approximation for formatting\n457\t    format!(\n458\t        \"{:04}-{:02}-{:02}T{:02}:{:02}:{:02}.{:03}Z\",\n459\t        1970 + days_since_epoch / 365,\n460\t        1 + (days_since_epoch % 365) / 30,\n461\t        1 + (days_since_epoch % 30),\n462\t        hours,\n463\t        minutes,\n464\t        seconds,\n465\t        millis_part\n466\t    )\n467\t}\n468\t\n469\t#[cfg(test)]\n470\tmod tests {\n471\t    use super::*;\n472\t    use }\n565\t\n566\t    #[test]\n567\t    fn test_format_millis_timestamp() {\n568\t        let ts = format_millis_timestamp(1700000000000);\n569\t        assert!(ts.contains(\"T\"));\n570\t        assert!(ts.contains(\"Z\"));\n571\t    }\n572\t\n573\t    #[tokio::test(flavor = \"multi_thread\")]\n574\t    async fn test_task_registry_impl() {\n575\t        let registry =     let filter = TaskFilter::default();\n594\t        let tasks = registry.list(filter).unwrap();\n595\t        assert_eq!(tasks.len(), 1);\n596\t    }\n597\t}\n598\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"f0e9813c-671d-482b-b16d-291c26a00e42","uuid":"c434337f-2656-45cc-a862-b5e66c50e7a7","timestamp":"2026-05-24T02:53:33.290Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-proxy/src/routes/tasks.rs","content":"//! Task API endpoints: Miroir task namespace reconciliation.\n//!\n//! Implements P2.5 task reconciliation:\n//! - GET /tasks — List all Miroir tasks with Meilisearch-compatible filters (statuses, types, indexUids)\n//! - GET /tasks/{id} — Get task status by mtask ID with per-node breakdown (polls nodes on each request)\n//! - DELETE /tasks/{id} — Cancel a task (best-effort)\n\nuse axum::extract::{FromRef, Path, Query, State};\nuse axum::http::StatusCode;\nuse axum::{Json, Router};\nuse miroir_core::scatter::{NodeClient, TaskStatusRequest};\nuse miroir_core::task::{MiroirTask, TaskRegistry, TaskStatus, NodeTaskStatus};\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\n\nuse crate::client::HttpClient;\nuse crate::routes::admin_endpoints::AppState;\n\n/// Query parameters for GET /tasks (Meilisearch-compatible).\n#[derive(Debug, Deserialize)]\npub struct TasksQuery {\n    /// Filter by status (comma-separated: \"succeeded,failed\")\n    statuses: Option<String>,\n    /// Filter by index UID (comma-separated: \"index1,index2\")\n    indexUids: Option<String>,\n    /// Filter by type (comma-separated: \"documentAdditionOrUpdate,documentDeletion\")\n    types: Option<String>,\n    /// Pagination: limit number of results\n    limit: Option<usize>,\n    /// Pagination: offset from start\n    from: Option<usize>,\n}\n\n/// Meilisearch-compatible task response.\n#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]\npub struct TaskResponse {\n    #[serde(rename = \"taskUid\")]\n    pub task_uid: String,\n    pub indexUid: Option<String>,\n    pub status: String,\n    #[serde(rename = \"type\")]\n    pub task_type: Option<String>,\n    pub details: Option<TaskDetails>,\n    pub error: Option<TaskError>,\n    pub duration: Option<String>,\n    pub enqueuedAt: String,\n    pub startedAt: Option<String>,\n    pub finishedAt: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Task details with per-node breakdown.\n#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]\npub struct TaskDetails {\n    /// Number of documents received (for document operations)\n    pub received_documents: Option<usize>,\n    /// Per-node task mapping\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"HashMap::is_empty\")]\n    pub nodes: HashMap<String, NodeTaskDetail>,\n}\n\n/// Per-node task detail.\n#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]\npub struct NodeTaskDetail {\n    /// Local Meilisearch task UID on this node\n    #[serde(rename = \"taskUid\")]\n    pub task_uid: u64,\n    /// Status of this node task\n    pub status: String,\n}\n\n/// Task error information with per-node breakdown.\n#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]\npub struct TaskError {\n    pub code: String,\n    pub message: String,\n    #[serde(rename = \"type\")]\n    pub error_type: String,\n    /// Per-node error details\n    pub details: HashMap<String, String>,\n}\n\n/// Response for GET /tasks.\n#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]\npub struct TasksListResponse {\n    pub results: Vec<TaskResponse>,\n    pub limit: usize,\n    pub from: usize,\n    pub total: usize,\n}\n\n/// Build router for task endpoints.\npub fn router<S>() -> Router<S>\nwhere\n    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n{\n    Router::new()\n        .route(\"/\", axum::routing::get(list_tasks::<S>))\n        .route(\"/:id\", axum::routing::get(get_task::<S>))\n        .route(\"/:id\", axum::routing::delete(delete_task::<S>))\n}\n\n/// GET /tasks — List all Miroir tasks with optional filtering.\nasync fn list_tasks<S>(\n    Query(query): Query<TasksQuery>,\n    State(state): State<S>,\n) -> Result<Json<TasksListResponse>, StatusCode>\nwhere\n    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n{\n    let state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n\n    // Parse status filter (supports comma-separated values, takes first)\n    let status_filter = query.statuses.as_ref().and_then(|s| {\n        s.split(',')\n            .next()\n            .and_then(|status_str| match status_str.trim() {\n                \"succeeded\" | \"Succeeded\" => Some(TaskStatus::Succeeded),\n                \"failed\" | \"Failed\" => Some(TaskStatus::Failed),\n                \"processing\" | \"Processing\" => Some(TaskStatus::Processing),\n                \"enqueued\" | \"Enqueued\" => Some(TaskStatus::Enqueued),\n                \"canceled\" | \"Canceled\" => Some(TaskStatus::Canceled),\n                _ => None,\n            })\n    });\n\n    // Parse indexUids filter (supports comma-separated values, takes first)\n    let index_uid_filter = query.indexUids.as_ref().and_then(|s| {\n        s.split(',')\n            .next()\n            .map(|uid| uid.trim().to_string())\n    });\n\n    // Parse types filter (supports comma-separated values, takes first)\n    let task_type_filter = query.types.as_ref().and_then(|s| {\n        s.split(',')\n            .next()\n            .map(|ty| ty.trim().to_string())\n    });\n\n    // Build filter with all parameters\n    let filter = miroir_core::task::TaskFilter {\n        status: status_filter,\n        node_id: None,\n        index_uid: index_uid_filter,\n        task_type: task_type_filter,\n        limit: query.limit,\n        offset: query.from,\n    };\n\n    // List tasks from registry\n    let tasks = state\n        .task_registry\n        .list(filter)\n        .map_err(|_| StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)?;\n\n    // Get total count (without limit/offset)\n    let total = state\n        .task_registry\n        .count();\n\n    // Convert to Meilisearch-compatible response\n    let results = tasks.into_iter().map(task_to_response).collect();\n\n    let limit = query.limit.unwrap_or(20);\n    let from = query.from.unwrap_or(0);\n\n    Ok(Json(TasksListResponse {\n        results,\n        limit,\n        from,\n        total,\n    }))\n}\n\n/// GET /tasks/{id} — Get a specific task by Miroir task ID.\n///\n/// Polls all mapped nodes for their current task status and aggregates the result.\nasync fn get_task<S>(\n    Path(id): Path<String>,\n    State(state): State<S>,\n) -> Result<Json<TaskResponse>, StatusCode>\nwhere\n    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n{\n    let state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n\n    // Validate task ID format\n    if !id.starts_with(\"mtask-\") {\n        return Err(StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST);\n    }\n\n    let mut task = state\n        .task_registry\n        .get(&id)\n        .map_err(|_| StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)?\n        .ok_or(StatusCode::NOT_FOUND)?;\n\n    // Poll nodes for current status if task is not terminal\n    if !matches!(task.status, TaskStatus::Succeeded | TaskStatus::Failed | TaskStatus::Canceled) {\n        let topology = state.topology.read().await;\n        let client = HttpClient::new(\n            state.config.node_master_key.clone(),\n            state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n        );\n\n        // Update node task statuses by polling each node\n        let mut node_errors = HashMap::new();\n        let mut any_processing = false;\n        let mut all_succeeded = true;\n        let mut any_failed = false;\n\n        for (node_id_str, node_task) in &task.node_tasks {\n            let node_id = miroir_core::topology::NodeId::new(node_id_str.clone());\n\n            // Skip polling if node task is already terminal\n            if matches!(node_task.status, NodeTaskStatus::Succeeded | NodeTaskStatus::Failed) {\n                if matches!(node_task.status, NodeTaskStatus::Failed) {\n                    any_failed = true;\n                    all_succeeded = false;\n                }\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // Get node address from topology\n            let node = match topology.node(&node_id) {\n                Some(n) => n,\n                None => {\n                    node_errors.insert(node_id_str.clone(), \"node not found in topology\".to_string());\n                    any_failed = true;\n                    all_succeeded = false;\n                    continue;\n                }\n            };\n\n            // Poll this node for task status\n            let req = TaskStatusRequest { task_uid: node_task.task_uid };\n            match client.get_task_status(&node_id, &node.address, &req).await {\n                Ok(resp) => {\n                    let new_status = resp.to_node_status();\n                    // Update the node task status in the registry\n                    let _ = state.task_registry.update_node_task(&id, node_id_str, new_status);\n\n                    // Track overall status\n                    match new_status {\n                        NodeTaskStatus::Succeeded => {}\n                        NodeTaskStatus::Failed => {\n                            any_failed = true;\n                            all_succeeded = false;\n                            if let Some(error) = resp.error {\n                                node_errors.insert(node_id_str.clone(), error);\n                            }\n                        }\n                        NodeTaskStatus::Processing => {\n                            any_processing = true;\n                            all_succeeded = false;\n                        }\n                        NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued => {\n                            all_succeeded = false;\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    tracing::warn!(node = %node_id_str, task = %id, error = ?e, \"failed to poll node for task\");\n                    // Don't mark as failed on network error - may be transient\n                    all_succeeded = false;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Update overall task status based on node task statuses\n        let new_status = if any_failed {\n            TaskStatus::Failed\n        } else if all_succeeded {\n            TaskStatus::Succeeded\n        } else if any_processing {\n            TaskStatus::Processing\n        } else {\n            TaskStatus::Enqueued\n        };\n\n        // Record terminal task status in Prometheus (§10 task metrics)\n        if matches!(new_status, TaskStatus::Succeeded | TaskStatus::Failed | TaskStatus::Canceled) {\n            let status_str = match new_status {\n                TaskStatus::Succeeded => \"succeeded\",\n                TaskStatus::Failed => \"failed\",\n                TaskStatus::Canceled => \"canceled\",\n                _ => unreachable!(),\n            };\n            state.metrics.inc_tasks_total(status_str);\n\n            // Observe task processing age (time from creation to terminal state)\n            let age_ms = {\n                let now_ms = std::time::SystemTime::now()\n                    .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n                    .unwrap_or_default()\n                    .as_millis() as u64;\n                now_ms.saturating_sub(task.created_at)\n            };\n            state.metrics.observe_task_processing_age(age_ms as f64 / 1000.0);\n        }\n\n        // Update the task status in the registry\n        let _ = state.task_registry.update_status(&id, new_status);\n\n        // Update the task with node errors and new status\n        task.status = new_status;\n        task.node_errors = node_errors;\n\n        // Set timestamps\n        if matches!(new_status, TaskStatus::Processing) && task.started_at.is_none() {\n            task.started_at = Some(std::time::SystemTime::now()\n                .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n                .unwrap_or_default()\n                .as_millis() as u64);\n        }\n\n        if matches!(new_status, TaskStatus::Succeeded | TaskStatus::Failed | TaskStatus::Canceled) && task.finished_at.is_none() {\n            task.finished_at = Some(std::time::SystemTime::now()\n                .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n                .unwrap_or_default()\n                .as_millis() as u64);\n        }\n    }\n\n    Ok(Json(task_to_response(task)))\n}\n\n/// DELETE /tasks/{id} — Cancel a task (best-effort).\nasync fn delete_task<S>(\n    Path(id): Path<String>,\n    State(state): State<S>,\n) -> Result<Json<TaskResponse>, StatusCode>\nwhere\n    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n{\n    let state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n\n    // Validate task ID format\n    if !id.starts_with(\"mtask-\") {\n        return Err(StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST);\n    }\n\n    // Get the task first\n    let task = state\n        .task_registry\n        .get(&id)\n        .map_err(|_| StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)?\n        .ok_or(StatusCode::NOT_FOUND)?;\n\n    // Update status to canceled if not already terminal\n    if matches!(task.status, TaskStatus::Enqueued | TaskStatus::Processing) {\n        state\n            .task_registry\n            .update_status(&id, TaskStatus::Canceled)\n            .map_err(|_| StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)?;\n    }\n\n    // Return the updated task\n    let updated = state\n        .task_registry\n        .get(&id)\n        .map_err(|_| StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)?\n        .ok_or(StatusCode::NOT_FOUND)?;\n\n    Ok(Json(task_to_response(updated)))\n}\n\n/// Convert MiroirTask to Meilisearch-compatible TaskResponse.\nfn task_to_response(task: MiroirTask) -> TaskResponse {\n    let status_str = match task.status {\n        TaskStatus::Enqueued => \"enqueued\",\n        TaskStatus::Processing => \"processing\",\n        TaskStatus::Succeeded => \"succeeded\",\n        TaskStatus::Failed => \"failed\",\n        TaskStatus::Canceled => \"canceled\",\n    };\n\n    let enqueued_at = format_millis_timestamp(task.created_at);\n    let started_at = task.started_at.map(|t| format_millis_timestamp(t));\n    let finished_at = task.finished_at.map(|t| format_millis_timestamp(t));\n\n    let error = if task.status == TaskStatus::Failed {\n        Some(TaskError {\n            code: \"internal_error\".to_string(),\n            message: task.error.clone().unwrap_or_else(|| {\n                if task.node_errors.is_empty() {\n                    \"task failed\".to_string()\n                } else {\n                    format!(\"{} node(s) failed\", task.node_errors.len())\n                }\n            }),\n            error_type: \"internal_error\".to_string(),\n            details: task.node_errors.clone(),\n        })\n    } else {\n        None\n    };\n\n    // Build per-node details\n    let mut nodes = HashMap::new();\n    for (node_id, node_task) in &task.node_tasks {\n        let node_status = match node_task.status {\n            miroir_core::task::NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued => \"enqueued\",\n            miroir_core::task::NodeTaskStatus::Processing => \"processing\",\n            miroir_core::task::NodeTaskStatus::Succeeded => \"succeeded\",\n            miroir_core::task::NodeTaskStatus::Failed => \"failed\",\n        };\n        nodes.insert(\n            node_id.clone(),\n            NodeTaskDetail {\n                task_uid: node_task.task_uid,\n                status: node_status.to_string(),\n            },\n        );\n    }\n\n    let details = Some(TaskDetails {\n        received_documents: None,\n        nodes,\n    });\n\n    TaskResponse {\n        task_uid: task.miroir_id,\n        indexUid: task.index_uid,\n        status: status_str.to_string(),\n        task_type: task.task_type,\n        details,\n        error,\n        duration: None,\n        enqueuedAt: enqueued_at,\n        startedAt: started_at,\n        finishedAt: finished_at,\n    }\n}\n\n/// Format milliseconds since epoch as ISO 8601 timestamp.\nfn format_millis_timestamp(millis: u64) -> String {\n    // Simple ISO 8601 format without chrono dependency\n    let secs = millis / 1000;\n    let millis_part = millis % 1000;\n\n    // Calculate date components (simplified, assumes Unix epoch)\n    // This is a rough approximation - for production use chrono or time crate\n    let days_since_epoch = secs / 86400;\n    let seconds_in_day = secs % 86400;\n\n    let hours = seconds_in_day / 3600;\n    let minutes = (seconds_in_day % 3600) / 60;\n    let seconds = seconds_in_day % 60;\n\n    // Days from 1970-01-01 to 2000-01-01 is roughly 10957 days\n    // This is a very rough approximation for formatting\n    format!(\n        \"{:04}-{:02}-{:02}T{:02}:{:02}:{:02}.{:03}Z\",\n        1970 + days_since_epoch / 365,\n        1 + (days_since_epoch % 365) / 30,\n        1 + (days_since_epoch % 30),\n        hours,\n        minutes,\n        seconds,\n        millis_part\n    )\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use miroir_core::task::{NodeTask, NodeTaskStatus, TaskFilter};\n    use miroir_core::task_registry::TaskRegistryImpl;\n    use std::collections::HashMap;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn Update the overall task status based on node task statuses.\n251\t    pub async fn update_overall_status(&self, miroir_id: &str) -> Result<bool> {\n252\t        let mut tasks = self.tasks.write().await;\n253\t        let task = match tasks.get(miroir_id) {\n254\t            Some(t) => t.clone(),\n255\t            None => return Ok(false),\n256\t        };\n257\t\n258\t TaskStatus::Failed\n278\t        } else if all_terminal && all_succeeded {\n279\t            TaskStatus::Succeeded\n280\t        } else if !all_terminal {\n281\t            TaskStatus::Processing\n282\t        } else {\n283\t            TaskStatus::Enqueued\n284\t        };\n285\t\n286\t        if new_status != task.status {\n287\t            if let Some(t) = }\n295\t\n296\t    /// Poll node tasks to update the overall Miroir task status.\n297\t    /// Uses exponential backoff: 25ms → 50 → 100 → ... → 1s cap.\n298\t    ///\n299\t    /// Phase 2: Simulates node polling (tasks complete after ~500ms)\n300\t    /// Phase 3: Will poll actual nodes via HttpClient using topology\n301\t    async fn poll_task_status_simulated(&self, miroir_id: &str) {\n302\t        let mut delay_ms = 25u64;\n303\t        let max_delay_ms = 1000u64;\n304\t\n305\t        loop {\n306\t            // Get the current task state\n307\t            let task = self.get_async(miroir_id).await;\n308\t\n309\t            let task = match task {\n310\t                Some(t) => t,\n311\t                None => return, // Task was deleted\n312\t            };\n313\t\n314\t            // Check if we've reached a terminal state\n315\t            if matches!(task.status, TaskStatus::Succeeded | TaskStatus::Failed | TaskStatus::Canceled) {\n316\t                return;\n317\t            }\n318\t\n319\t            // In a real implementation, we would query the nodes here\n320\t            // For Phase 2, we simulate status progression\n321\t            // Phase 3 will add actual node polling via HttpClient\n322\t\n323\t            // Check each node task's status\n324\t            let mut all_terminal = true;\n325\t            for (_node_id, node_task) in &task.node_tasks {\n326\t                match node_task.status {\n327\t                    NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued | NodeTaskStatus::Processing => {\n328\t                        all_terminal = false;\n329\t                    }\n330\t                    NodeTaskStatus::Succeeded | NodeTaskStatus::Failed => {}\n331\t                }\n332\t            }\n333\t\n334\t            // For testing purposes, simulate tasks completing\n335\t            // In production, this would poll actual nodes\n336\t            if !all_terminal && delay_ms >= 500 {\n337\t                // Simulate completion for testing\n338\t                let mut tasks = self.tasks.write().await;\n339\t                if let Some(t) = tasks.get_mut(miroir_id) {\n340\t                    for (_node_id, node_task) in &mut t.node_tasks {\n341\t                        if matches!(node_task.status, NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued | NodeTaskStatus::Processing) {\n342\t                            node_task.status = NodeTaskStatus::Succeeded;\n343\t                        }\n344\t                    }\n345\t                    // Update overall status\n346\t                    let mut all_succeeded = true;\n347\t                    let mut any_failed = false;\n348\t                    for (_node_id, node_task) in &t.node_tasks {\n349\t                        match node_task.status {\n350\t                            NodeTaskStatus::Succeeded => {}\n351\t                            NodeTaskStatus::Failed => any_failed = true,\n352\t                            NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued | NodeTaskStatus::Processing => {\n353\t                                all_succeeded = false;\n354\t                            }\n355\t                        }\n356\t                    }\n357\t                    if any_failed {\n358\t                        t.status = TaskStatus::Failed;\n359\t                    } else if all_succeeded {\n360\t                        t.status = TaskStatus::Succeeded;\n361\t                    } else {\n362\t                        t.status = TaskStatus::Processing;\n363\t                    }\n364\t                    // Set finished timestamp for terminal states\n365\t                    if matches!(t.status, TaskStatus::Succeeded | TaskStatus::Failed | TaskStatus::Canceled) {\n366\t                        t.finished_at = Some(std::time::SystemTime::now()\n367\t                            .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n368\t                            .unwrap_or_default()\n369\t                            .as_millis() as u64);\n370\t                    }\n371\t                }\n372\t                return;\n373\t            }\n374\t\n375\t            // Exponential backoff with cap\n376\t            tokio::time::sleep(std::time::Duration::from_millis(delay_ms)).await;\n377\t            delay_ms = (delay_ms * 2).min(max_delay_ms);\n378\t        }\n379\t    }\n380\t\n381\t    /// Poll node tasks to update the overall Miroir task status, using real node polling.\n382\t    /// Uses exponential backoff: 25ms → 50 → 100 → ... → 1s cap.\n383\t    async fn poll_task_status_with_poller<P: NodePoller>(&self, miroir_id: &str, poller: Arc<P>) {\n384\t        let mut delay_ms = 25u64;\n385\t        let max_delay_ms = 1000u64;\n386\t\n387\t        loop {\n388\t            // Get the current task state\n389\t            let task = self.get_async(miroir_id).await;\n390\t\n391\t            let task = match task {\n392\t                Some(t) => t,\n393\t                None => return, // Task was deleted\n394\t            };\n395\t\n396\t            // Check if we've reached a terminal state\n397\t            if matches!(task.status, TaskStatus::Succeeded | TaskStatus::Failed | TaskStatus::Canceled) {\n398\t                return;\n399\t            }\n400\t\n401\t            // Collect node IDs and task UIDs to poll\n402\t            let node_polls: Vec<(NodeId, u64)> = task.node_tasks\n403\t                .iter()\n404\t                .filter(|(_, nt)| !matches!(nt.status, NodeTaskStatus::Succeeded | NodeTaskStatus::Failed))\n405\t                .map(|(node_id, nt)| (NodeId::new(node_id.clone()), nt.task_uid))\n406\t                .collect();\n407\t\n408\t            if node_polls.is_empty() {\n409\t                // All node tasks are terminal, update overall status\n410\t                let mut tasks = self.tasks.write().await;\n411\t                if let Some(t) = tasks.get_mut(miroir_id) {\n412\t                    let mut all_succeeded = true;\n413\t                    let mut any_failed = false;\n414\t                    for (_node_id, node_task) in &t.node_tasks {\n415\t                        match node_task.status {\n416\t                            NodeTaskStatus::Succeeded => {}\n417\t                            NodeTaskStatus::Failed => any_failed = true,\n418\t                            NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued | NodeTaskStatus::Processing => {\n419\t                                all_succeeded = false;\n420\t                            }\n421\t                        }\n422\t                    }\n423\t                    if any_failed {\n424\t                        t.status = TaskStatus::Failed;\n425\t                    } else if all_succeeded {\n426\t                        t.status = TaskStatus::Succeeded;\n427\t                    } else {\n428\t                        t.status = TaskStatus::Processing;\n429\t                    }\n430\t                    // Set finished timestamp for terminal states\n431\t                    if matches!(t.status, TaskStatus::Succeeded | TaskStatus::Failed | TaskStatus::Canceled) {\n432\t                        t.finished_at = Some(std::time::SystemTime::now()\n433\t                            .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n434\t                            .unwrap_or_default()\n435\t                            .as_millis() as u64);\n436\t                    }\n437\t                }\n438\t                return;\n439\t            }\n440\t\n441\t            // Poll each node for status\n442\t            let mut node_statuses = HashMap::new();\n443\t            for (node_id, task_uid) in &node_polls {\n444\t                // Get node address from topology (would need topology here)\n445\t                // For now, use a mock address - in production, this would come from the topology\n446\t                let address = format!(\"http://{}\", node_id.as_str());\n447\t\n448\t                match poller.poll_node_task(&node_id, &address, *task_uid).await {\n449\t                    Ok(status) => {\n450\t                        node_statuses.insert(node_id.clone(), status);\n451\t                    }\n452\t                    Err(e) => {\n453\t                        tracing::warn!(\"Failed to poll node {} for task {}: {}\", node_id, task_uid, e);\n454\t                        // On poll failure, keep the current status but mark for potential degradation\n455\t                    }\n456\t                }\n457\t            }\n458\t\n459\t            // Update node task statuses\n460\t            {\n461\t                let mut tasks = self.tasks.write().await;\n462\t                if let Some(t) = tasks.get_mut(miroir_id) {\n463\t                    for (node_id, status) in node_statuses {\n464\t                        if let Some(node_task) = t.node_tasks.get_mut(node_id.as_str()) {\n465\t                            node_task.status = status;\n466\t                        }\n467\t                    }\n468\t\n469\t                    // Update started_at timestamp if moving to processing\n470\t                    if t.status == TaskStatus::Enqueued {\n471\t                        let any_processing = t.node_tasks.values().any(|nt| {\n472\t                            matches!(nt.status, NodeTaskStatus::Processing)\n473\t                        });\n474\t                        if any_processing && t.started_at.is_none() {\n475\t                            t.started_at = Some(std::time::SystemTime::now()\n476\t                                .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n477\t                                .unwrap_or_default()\n478\t                                .as_millis() as u64);\n479\t                            t.status = TaskStatus::Processing;\n480\t                        }\n481\t                    }\n482\t                }\n483\t            }\n484\t\n485\t            // Exponential backoff with cap\n486\t            tokio::time::sleep(std::time::Duration::from_millis(delay_ms)).await;\n487\t            delay_ms = (delay_ms * 2).min(max_delay_ms);\n488\t        }\n489\t    }\n490\t\n491\t    /// List tasks with optional filtering (Meilisearch-compatible).\n492\t    pub async fn list_async(&self, filter: &TaskFilter) -> Result<Vec<MiroirTask>> {\n493\t        let guard = self.tasks.read().await;\n494\t        let mut result: Vec<MiroirTask> = guard.values().cloned().collect();\n495\t\n496\t        // Apply status filter\n497\t        if let Some(status) = filter.status {\n498\t            result.retain(|t| t.status == status);\n499\t        }\n500\t\n501\t        // Apply index_uid filter\n502\t        if let Some(index_uid) = &filter.index_uid {\n503\t            result.retain(|t| t.index_uid.as_ref().map_or(false, |uid| uid == index_uid));\n504\t        }\n505\t\n506\t        // Apply task_type filter\n507\t        if let Some(task_type) = &filter.task_type {\n508\t            result.retain(|t| t.task_type.as_ref().map_or(false, |ty| ty == task_type));\n509\t        }\n510\t\n511\t        // Apply offset\n512\t        if let Some(offset) = filter.offset {\n513\t            if offset < result.len() {\n514\t                result = result[offset..].to_vec();\n515\t            } else {\n516\t                result.clear();\n517\t            }\n518\t        }\n519\t\n520\t        // Apply limit\n521\t        if let Some(limit) = filter.limit {\n522\t            result.truncate(limit);\n523\t        }\n524\t\n525\t        Ok(result)\n526\t    }\n527\t}\n528\t\n529\t/// TaskRegistryImpl — runtime-selected backend (Phase 3)\n676\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n677\t\n678\t/// Task status as lowercase string for store serialization.\n679\tfn status_to_str(s: TaskStatus) -> &'static str {\n680\t    match s {\n681\t        TaskStatus::Enqueued => \"enqueued\",\n682\t        TaskStatus::Processing => \"processing\",\n683\t        TaskStatus::Succeeded => \"succeeded\",\n684\t        TaskStatus::Failed => \"failed\",\n685\t        TaskStatus::Canceled => \"canceled\",\n686\t    }\n687\t}\n688\t\n689\t/// Parse task status from store string.\n690\tfn str_to_status(s: &str) -> TaskStatus {\n691\t    match s {\n692\t        \"succeeded\" => TaskStatus::Succeeded,\n693\t        \"failed\" => TaskStatus::Failed,\n694\t        \"processing\" => TaskStatus::Processing,\n695\t        \"canceled\" => TaskStatus::Canceled,\n696\t        _ => TaskStatus::Enqueued,\n697\t    }\n698\t}\n699\t\n700\t/// Convert a store `TaskRow` to a `MiroirTask`.\n701\t///\n702\t/// Per-node status is not persisted; all nodes are set to `Enqueued`.\n703\t/// The GET /tasks/{id} endpoint polls nodes to reconstruct per-node status.\n704\tfn row_to_task(row: crate::task_store::TaskRow) -> MiroirTask {\n705\t    let node_tasks: HashMap<String, NodeTask> = row\n706\t        .node_tasks\n707\t        .into_iter()\n708\t        .map(|(node_id, task_uid)| {\n709\t            (\n710\t                node_id,\n711\t                NodeTask {\n712\t                    task_uid,\n713\t                    status: NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued,\n714\t                },\n715\t            )\n716\t        })\n717\t        .collect();\n718\t\n719\t    MiroirTask {\n720\t u64),\n724\t        status: str_to_status(&row.status),\n725\t        index_uid: row.index_uid,\n726\t        task_type: row.task_type,\n727\t        node_tasks,\n728\t        error: row.error,\n729\t        node_errors: row.node_errors,\n730\t    }\n731\t}\n732\t\n733\t/// Convert a route `TaskFilter` to a store `TaskFilter`.\n734\tfn filter_to_store(f: &TaskFilter) -> crate::task_store::TaskFilter {\n735\t    crate::task_store::TaskFilter {\n736\t        status: f.status.map(|s| status_to_str(s).to_string()),\n737\t        index_uid: f.index_uid.clone(),\n738\t        task_type: f.task_type.clone(),\n739\t        limit: f.limit,\n740\t        offset: f.offset,\n741\t    }\n742\t}\n743\t\n744\t/// Runtime-selected task registry backend.\n745\t///\n746\t/// - `InMemory`: Phase 2 behaviour, tasks lost on restart (default for sqlite without path).\n747\t/// - `Sqlite`: persisted via `SqliteTaskStore`, survives restarts.\n748\t/// - `Redis`: persisted via `RedisTaskStore`, required for multi-replica HPA.\n749\t#[derive(Clone)]\n750\tpub enum TaskRegistryImpl {\n751\t    InMemory(InMemoryTaskRegistry),\n752\t {\n758\t    /// Open a SQLite-backed registry at the given path.\n759\t    pub fn sqlite(path: &str) -> Result<Self> {\n760\t        let store =       .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n865\t                    .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Task(format!(\"clock error: {}\", e)))?\n866\t                    .as_millis() as i64;\n867\t\n868\t                let new_task = crate::task_store::NewTask {\n869\t                    miroir_id: miroir_id.clone(),\n870\t                    created_at,\n871\t                    status: \"enqueued\".to_string(),\n872\t                    node_tasks,\n873\t                    error: None,\n874\t                    started_at: None,\n875\t                    finished_at: None,\n876\t                    index_uid,\n877\t                    task_type,\n878\t                    node_errors: HashMap::new(),\n879\t                };\n880\t\n881\t                store.insert_task(&new_task)?;\n882\t\n883\t                Ok(MiroirTask {\n884\t                    miroir_id: new_task.miroir_id,\n885\t                    created_at: new_task.created_at as u64,\n886\t                    started_at: None,\n887\t                    finished_at: None,\n888\t                    status: TaskStatus::Enqueued,\n889\t                    index_uid: new_task.index_uid,\n890\t                    task_type: new_task.task_type,\n891\t                    node_tasks: new_task\n892\t                        .node_tasks\n893\t                        .into_iter()\n894\t                        .map(|(nid, uid)| (nid, NodeTask { task_uid: uid, status: NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued }))\n895\t                        .collect(),\n896\t                    error: None,\n897\t                    node_errors: HashMap::new(),\n898\t                })\n899\t            }\n900\t        }\n901\t    }\n902\t\n903\t    /// Get a task by Miroir ID.\n904\t    pub     TaskRegistryImpl::Sqlite(store) => {\n908\t                Ok(store.get_task(miroir_id)?.map(row_to_task))\n909\t            }\n910\t            #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n911\t    }\n    }\n\n    /// Register a new task with the given node tasks.\n    pub async fn register_async(&self, node_tasks: HashMap<String, u64>) -> Result<MiroirTask> {\n &self,\n        node_tasks: HashMap<String, u64>,\n        index_uid: Option<String>,\n        task_type: Option<String>,\n    ) -> Result<MiroirTask> {\n        let miroir_id = format!(\"mtask-{}\", Uuid::new_v4());\n        let created_at = std::time::SystemTime::now()\n            .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Task(format!(\"clock error: {}\", e)))?\n            .as_millis() as u64;\n\n        let mut tasks = HashMap::new();\n        for (node_id, task_uid) in node_tasks {\n            tasks.insert(node_id, NodeTask {\n                task_uid,\n                status: NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued,\n            });\n        }\n\n        let task = MiroirTask {\n            miroir_id: miroir_id.clone(),\n            created_at,\n            started_at: None,\n            finished_at: None,\n            status: TaskStatus::Enqueued,\n            index_uid,\n            task_type,\n            node_tasks: tasks,\n            error: None,\n            node_errors: HashMap::new(),\n        };\n\n        // Insert the task\n        {\n            let mut registry = self.tasks.write().await;\n            registry.insert(miroir_id.clone(), task.clone());\n        }\n\n        // Spawn a background task to poll for status updates (simulated for Phase 2)\n        let registry = self.clone();\n        let fn count(&self) -> usize {\n        let tasks = self.tasks.read().await;\n        tasks.len()\n    }\n\n    /// Update the status of a task (async version for tests).\n    /// Automatically sets started_at when transitioning to Processing.\n    /// Automatically sets finished_at when transitioning to a terminal state.\n    pub async fn update_status(&self, miroir_id: &str, status: TaskStatus) -> Result<()> {\n        let mut tasks = self.tasks.write().await;\n        if let Some(task) = tasks.get_mut(miroir_id) {\n            // Set started_at when transitioning to Processing\n            if status == TaskStatus::Processing && task.started_at.is_none() {\n                task.started_at = Some(std::time::SystemTime::now()\n                    .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n                    .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Task(format!(\"clock error: {}\", e)))?\n                    .as_millis() as u64);\n            }\n            // Set finished_at when transitioning to a terminal state\n            if matches!(status, TaskStatus::Succeeded | TaskStatus::Failed | TaskStatus::Canceled)\n                && task.finished_at.is_none() {\n                task.finished_at =  .as_millis() as u64);\n            }\n            task.status = status;\n        }\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Update a node task's status (async version for tests).\n    pub async fn update_node_task(\n        &self,\n        miroir_id: &str,\n        node_id: &str,\n        node_status: NodeTaskStatus,\n    ) -> Result<()> {\n        let mut tasks = self.tasks.write().await;\n        if let Some(task) = tasks.get_mut(miroir_id) {\n            if let Some(node_task) = task.node_tasks.get_mut(node_id) {\n                node_task.status = node_status;\n            }\n        }\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Prune old tasks (in-memory only, for Phase 3 this will use durable storage).\n    pub async fn prune_old_tasks(&self, _cutoff_ms: u64) -> Result<usize> {\n        //   pub async fn update_overall_status(&self, miroir_id: &str) -> Result<bool> {\n        let mut tasks = self.tasks.write().await;\n        let task = match tasks.get(miroir_id) {\n            Some(t) => t.clone(),\n            None => return Ok(false),\n        };\n\n        // Determine overall status from node tasks\n        let mut all_succeeded = true;\n        let mut any_failed = false;\n        let mut all_terminal = true;\n\n        for (_node_id, node_task) in &task.node_tasks {\n            match node_task.status {\n                NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued | NodeTaskStatus::Processing => {\n                    all_terminal = false;\n                    all_succeeded = false;\n                }\n                NodeTaskStatus::Succeeded => {}\n                NodeTaskStatus::Failed => {\n                    any_failed = true;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        let new_status = if any_failed {\n            TaskStatus::Failed\n        } else if all_terminal && all_succeeded {\n            TaskStatus::Succeeded\n        } else if !all_terminal {\n            TaskStatus::Processing\n        } else {\n            TaskStatus::Enqueued\n        };\n\n        if new_status != task.status {\n            if let Some(t) = tasks.get_mut(miroir_id) {\n                t.status = new_status;\n            }\n            Ok(true)\n        } else {\n            Ok(false)\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Poll node tasks to update the overall Miroir task status.\n    /// Uses exponential backoff: 25ms → 50 → 100 → ... → 1s cap.\n    ///\n    /// Phase 2: Simulates node polling (tasks complete after ~500ms)\n    /// Phase 3: Will poll actual nodes via HttpClient using topology\n    async fn poll_task_status_simulated(&self, miroir_id: &str) {\n        let mut delay_ms = 25u64;\n        let max_delay_ms = 1000u64;\n\n        loop {\n            // Get the current task state\n            let task = self.get_async(miroir_id).await;\n\n            let task = match task {\n                Some(t) => t,\n                None => return, // Task was deleted\n            };\n\n            // Check if we've reached a terminal state\n            if matches!(task.status, TaskStatus::Succeeded | TaskStatus::Failed | TaskStatus::Canceled) {\n                return;\n            }\n\n            // In a real implementation, we would query the nodes here\n            // For Phase 2, we simulate status progression\n            // Phase 3 will add actual node polling via HttpClient\n\n            // Check each node task's status\n            let mut all_terminal = true;\n            for (_node_id, node_task) in &task.node_tasks {\n                match node_task.status {\n                    NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued | NodeTaskStatus::Processing => {\n                        all_terminal = false;\n                    }\n                    NodeTaskStatus::Succeeded | NodeTaskStatus::Failed => {}\n                }\n            }\n\n            // For testing purposes, simulate tasks completing\n            // In production, this would poll actual nodes\n            if !all_terminal && delay_ms >= 500 {\n                // Simulate completion for testing\n                let mut tasks = self.tasks.write().await;\n                if let Some(t) = tasks.get_mut(miroir_id) {\n                    for (_node_id, node_task) in &mut t.node_tasks {\n                        if matches!(node_task.status, NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued | NodeTaskStatus::Processing) {\n                            node_task.status = NodeTaskStatus::Succeeded;\n                        }\n                    }\n                    // Update overall status\n                    let mut all_succeeded = true;\n                    let mut any_failed = false;\n                    for (_node_id, node_task) in &t.node_tasks {\n                        match node_task.status {\n                            NodeTaskStatus::Succeeded => {}\n                            NodeTaskStatus::Failed => any_failed = true,\n                            NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued | NodeTaskStatus::Processing => {\n                                all_succeeded = false;\n                            }\n                        }\n                    }\n                    if any_failed {\n                        t.status = TaskStatus::Failed;\n                    } else if all_succeeded {\n                        t.status = TaskStatus::Succeeded;\n                    } else {\n                        t.status = TaskStatus::Processing;\n                    }\n                    // Set finished timestamp for terminal states\n                    if matches!(t.status, TaskStatus::Succeeded | TaskStatus::Failed | TaskStatus::Canceled) {\n                        t.finished_at = Some(std::time::SystemTime::now()\n                            .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n                            .unwrap_or_default()\n                            .as_millis() as u64);\n                    }\n                }\n                return;\n            }\n\n            // Exponential backoff with cap\n            tokio::time::sleep(std::time::Duration::from_millis(delay_ms)).await;\n            delay_ms = (delay_ms * 2).min(max_delay_ms);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Poll node tasks to update the overall Miroir task status, using real node polling.\n    /// Uses exponential backoff: 25ms → 50 → 100 → ... → 1s cap.\n    async fn poll_task_status_with_poller<P: NodePoller>(&self, miroir_id: &str, poller: Arc<P>) {\n        let mut delay_ms = 25u64;\n        let max_delay_ms = 1000u64;\n\n        loop {\n            // Get the current task state\n            let task = self.get_async(miroir_id).await;\n\n            let task = match task {\n                Some(t) => t,\n                None => return, // Task was deleted\n            };\n\n            // Check if we've reached a terminal state\n            if matches!(task.status, TaskStatus::Succeeded | TaskStatus::Failed | TaskStatus::Canceled) {\n                return;\n            }\n\n            // Collect node IDs and task UIDs to poll\n            let node_polls: Vec<(NodeId, u64)> = task.node_tasks\n                .iter()\n                .filter(|(_, nt)| !matches!(nt.status, NodeTaskStatus::Succeeded | NodeTaskStatus::Failed))\n                .map(|(node_id, nt)| (NodeId::new(node_id.clone()), nt.task_uid))\n                .collect();\n\n            if node_polls.is_empty() {\n                // All node tasks are terminal, update overall status\n                let mut tasks = self.tasks.write().await;\n                if let Some(t) = tasks.get_mut(miroir_id) {\n                    let mut all_succeeded = true;\n                    let mut any_failed = false;\n                    for (_node_id, node_task) in &t.node_tasks {\n                        match node_task.status {\n                            NodeTaskStatus::Succeeded => {}\n                            NodeTaskStatus::Failed => any_failed = true,\n                            NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued | NodeTaskStatus::Processing => {\n                                all_succeeded = false;\n                            }\n                        }\n                    }\n                    if any_failed {\n                        t.status = TaskStatus::Failed;\n                    } else if all_succeeded {\n                        t.status = TaskStatus::Succeeded;\n                    } else {\n                        t.status = TaskStatus::Processing;\n                    }\n                    // Set finished timestamp for terminal states\n                    if matches!(t.status, TaskStatus::Succeeded | TaskStatus::Failed | TaskStatus::Canceled) {\n                        t.finished_at = Some(std::time::SystemTime::now()\n                            .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n                            .unwrap_or_default()\n                            .as_millis() as u64);\n                    }\n                }\n                return;\n            }\n\n            // Poll each node for status\n            let mut node_statuses = HashMap::new();\n            for (node_id, task_uid) in &node_polls {\n                // Get node address from topology (would need topology here)\n                // For now, use a mock address - in production, this would come from the topology\n                let address = format!(\"http://{}\", node_id.as_str());\n\n                match poller.poll_node_task(&node_id, &address, *task_uid).await {\n                    Ok(status) => {\n                        node_statuses.insert(node_id.clone(), status);\n                    }\n                    Err(e) => {\n                        tracing::warn!(\"Failed to poll node {} for task {}: {}\", node_id, task_uid, e);\n                        // On poll failure, keep the current status but mark for potential degradation\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n\n            // Update node task statuses\n            {\n                let mut tasks = self.tasks.write().await;\n                if let Some(t) = tasks.get_mut(miroir_id) {\n                    for (node_id, status) in node_statuses {\n                        if let Some(node_task) = t.node_tasks.get_mut(node_id.as_str()) {\n                            node_task.status = status;\n                        }\n                    }\n\n                    // Update started_at timestamp if moving to processing\n                    if t.status == TaskStatus::Enqueued {\n                        let any_processing = t.node_tasks.values().any(|nt| {\n                            matches!(nt.status, NodeTaskStatus::Processing)\n                        });\n                        if any_processing && t.started_at.is_none() {\n                            t.started_at = Some(std::time::SystemTime::now()\n                                .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n                                .unwrap_or_default()\n                                .as_millis() as u64);\n                            t.status = TaskStatus::Processing;\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n\n            // Exponential backoff with cap\n            tokio::time::sleep(std::time::Duration::from_millis(delay_ms)).await;\n            delay_ms = (delay_ms * 2).min(max_delay_ms);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// List tasks with optional filtering (Meilisearch-compatible).\n    pub async fn list_async(&self, filter: &TaskFilter) -> Result<Vec<MiroirTask>> {\n        let guard = self.tasks.read().await;\n        let mut result: Vec<MiroirTask> = guard.values().cloned().collect();\n\n        // Apply status filter\n        if let Some(status) = filter.status {\n            result.retain(|t| t.status == status);\n        }\n\n        // Apply index_uid filter\n        if let Some(index_uid) = &filter.index_uid {\n            result.retain(|t| t.index_uid.as_ref().map_or(false, |uid| uid == index_uid));\n        }\n\n        // Apply task_type filter\n        if let Some(task_type) = &filter.task_type {\n            result.retain(|t| t.task_type.as_ref().map_or(false, |ty| ty == task_type));\n        }\n\n        // Apply offset\n        if let Some(offset) = filter.offset {\n            if offset < result.len() {\n                result = result[offset..].to_vec();\n            } else {\n                result.clear();\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Apply limit\n        if let Some(limit) = filter.limit {\n            result.truncate(limit);\n        }\n\n        Ok(result)\n    }\n}\n\n/// Test helper: set error on a task (for testing failure scenarios).\n#[cfg(feature = \"test-helpers\")]\nimpl if let Some(t) = tasks.get_mut(miroir_id) {\n            t.error = Some(error);\n            t.node_errors = node_errors;\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub async fn set_timestamps_for_test(&self,
