}\n\n    /// Test helper: set the status of a specific node task.\n    pub async fn set_node_task_status_for_test(&self, miroir_id: &str, node_id: &str, status: NodeTaskStatus) {\n        let mut tasks = self.tasks.write().await;\n        if let Some(t) = tasks.get_mut(miroir_id) {\n            if let Some(nt) = t.node_tasks.get_mut(node_id) {\n                nt.status = status;\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Test helper: set the overall task status.\n    pub async fn set_task_status_for_test(&self, miroir_id: &str, status: TaskStatus) {\n        let mut tasks = self.tasks.write().await;\n        if let Some(t) = tasks.get_mut(miroir_id) {\n            t.status = status;\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Default for InMemoryTaskRegistry {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::new()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Stub TaskRegistry implementation for compatibility.\n/// This delegates to the async methods via tokio::task::block_in_place.\n#[async_trait::async_trait]\nimpl crate::task::TaskRegistry for InMemoryTaskRegistry {\n    fn register_with_metadata(\n        &self,\n        node_tasks: HashMap<String, u64>,\n        index_uid: Option<String>,\n        task_type: Option<String>,\n    ) -> Result<MiroirTask> {\n        let registry = self.clone();\n        tokio::task::block_in_place(|| {\n            let rt = tokio::runtime::Handle::try_current()\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Task(format!(\"runtime error: {}\", e)))?;\n            rt.block_on(async move {\n                registry.register_async_with_metadata(node_tasks, index_uid, task_type).await\n            })\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn get(&self, miroir_id: &str) -> Result<Option<MiroirTask>> {\n        let registry = self.clone();\n        let miroir_id = miroir_id.to_string();\n        tokio::task::block_in_place(|| {\n            let rt = tokio::runtime::Handle::try_current()\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Task(format!(\"runtime error: {}\", e)))?;\n            rt.block_on(async move {\n                Ok(registry.get_async(&miroir_id).await)\n            })\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn update_status(&self, miroir_id: &str, status: TaskStatus) -> Result<()> {\n        let registry = self.clone();\n        let miroir_id = miroir_id.to_string();\n        tokio::task::block_in_place(|| {\n            let rt = tokio::runtime::Handle::try_current()\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Task(format!(\"runtime error: {}\", e)))?;\n            rt.block_on(async move {\n                let mut tasks = registry.tasks.write().await;\n                if let Some(task) = tasks.get_mut(&miroir_id) {\n                    task.status = status;\n                }\n                Ok(())\n            })\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn update_node_task(\n        &self,\n        miroir_id: &str,\n        node_id: &str,\n        node_status: NodeTaskStatus,\n    ) -> Result<()> {\n        let registry = self.clone();\n        let miroir_id = miroir_id.to_string();\n        let node_id = node_id.to_string();\n        tokio::task::block_in_place(|| {\n            let rt = tokio::runtime::Handle::try_current()\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Task(format!(\"runtime error: {}\", e)))?;\n            rt.block_on(async move {\n                let mut tasks = registry.tasks.write().await;\n                if let Some(task) = tasks.get_mut(&miroir_id) {\n                    if let Some(node_task) = task.node_tasks.get_mut(&node_id) {\n                        node_task.status = node_status;\n                    }\n                }\n                Ok(())\n            })\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn list(&self, filter: TaskFilter) -> Result<Vec<MiroirTask>> {\n        let registry = self.clone();\n        tokio::task::block_in_place(|| {\n            let rt = tokio::runtime::Handle::try_current()\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Task(format!(\"runtime error: {}\", e)))?;\n            rt.block_on(async move {\n                registry.list_async(&filter).await\n            })\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn count(&self) -> usize {\n        let registry = self.clone();\n        tokio::task::block_in_place(|| {\n            let rt = match tokio::runtime::Handle::try_current() {\n                Ok(rt) => rt,\n                Err(_) => return 0,\n            };\n            rt.block_on(async move {\n                registry.count().await\n            })\n        })\n    }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// TaskRegistryImpl — runtime-selected backend (Phase 3)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Task status as lowercase string for store serialization.\nfn status_to_str(s: TaskStatus) -> &'static str {\n    match s {\n        TaskStatus::Enqueued => \"enqueued\",\n        TaskStatus::Processing => \"processing\",\n        TaskStatus::Succeeded => \"succeeded\",\n        TaskStatus::Failed => \"failed\",\n        TaskStatus::Canceled => \"canceled\",\n    }\n}\n\n/// Parse task status from store string.\nfn str_to_status(s: &str) -> TaskStatus {\n    match s {\n        \"succeeded\" => TaskStatus::Succeeded,\n        \"failed\" => TaskStatus::Failed,\n        \"processing\" => TaskStatus::Processing,\n        \"canceled\" => TaskStatus::Canceled,\n        _ => TaskStatus::Enqueued,\n    }\n}\n\n/// Convert a store `TaskRow` to a `MiroirTask`.\n///\n/// Per-node status is not persisted; all nodes are set to `Enqueued`.\n/// The GET /tasks/{id} endpoint polls nodes to reconstruct per-node status.\nfn row_to_task(row: crate::task_store::TaskRow) -> MiroirTask {\n    let node_tasks: HashMap<String, NodeTask> = row\n        .node_tasks\n        .into_iter()\n        .map(|(node_id, task_uid)| {\n            (\n                node_id,\n                NodeTask {\n                    task_uid,\n                    status: NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued,\n                },\n            )\n        })\n        .collect();\n\n    MiroirTask {\n        miroir_id: row.miroir_id,\n        created_at: row.created_at as u64,\n        started_at: row.started_at.map(|t| t as u64),\n        finished_at: row.finished_at.map(|t| t as u64),\n        status: str_to_status(&row.status),\n        index_uid: row.index_uid,\n        task_type: row.task_type,\n        node_tasks,\n        error: row.error,\n        node_errors: row.node_errors,\n    }\n}\n\n/// Convert a route `TaskFilter` to a store `TaskFilter`.\nfn filter_to_store(f: &TaskFilter) -> crate::task_store::TaskFilter {\n    crate::task_store::TaskFilter {\n        status: f.status.map(|s| status_to_str(s).to_string()),\n        index_uid: f.index_uid.clone(),\n        task_type: f.task_type.clone(),\n        limit: f.limit,\n        offset: f.offset,\n    }\n}\n\n/// Runtime-selected task registry backend.\n///\n/// - `InMemory`: Phase 2 behaviour, tasks lost on restart (default for sqlite without path).\n/// - `Sqlite`: persisted via `SqliteTaskStore`, survives restarts.\n/// - `Redis`: persisted via `RedisTaskStore`, required for multi-replica HPA.\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub enum TaskRegistryImpl {\n    InMemory(InMemoryTaskRegistry),\n    Sqlite(Arc<crate::task_store::SqliteTaskStore>),\n    #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n    Redis(Arc<crate::task_store::RedisTaskStore>),\n}\n\nimpl TaskRegistryImpl {\n    /// Open a SQLite-backed registry at the given path.\n    pub fn sqlite(path: &str) -> Result<Self> {\n        let store = crate::task_store::SqliteTaskStore::open(std::path::Path::new(path))?;\n        store.migrate()?;\n        Ok(Self::Sqlite(Arc::new(store)))\n    }\n\n    /// Open a Redis-backed registry at the given URL.\n    #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n    pub fn redis(url: &str) -> Result<Self> {\n        let store = tokio::task::block_in_place(|| {\n            tokio::runtime::Handle::current()\n                .block_on(crate::task_store::RedisTaskStore::open(url))\n        })?;\n        store.migrate()?;\n        Ok(Self::Redis(Arc::new(store)))\n    }\n\n    /// In-memory fallback (no persistence).\n    pub fn in_memory() -> Self {\n        Self::InMemory(InMemoryTaskRegistry::new())\n    }\n\n    /// Return a reference to the underlying `SqliteTaskStore`, if this is the Sqlite variant.\n    pub fn as_sqlite(&self) -> Option<&Arc<crate::task_store::SqliteTaskStore>> {\n        match self {\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Sqlite(s) => Some(s),\n            _ => None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Return a reference to the underlying `RedisTaskStore`, if this is the Redis variant.\n    #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n    pub fn as_redis(&self) -> Option<&Arc<crate::task_store::RedisTaskStore>> {\n        match self {\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Redis(r) => Some(r),\n            _ => None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Return a reference to the underlying `TaskStore` trait object, if this is a persisted variant.\n    pub fn as_task_store(&self) -> Option<Arc<dyn crate::task_store::TaskStore>> {\n        match self {\n            TaskRegistryImpl::InMemory(_) => None,\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Sqlite(s) => Some(s.clone() as Arc<dyn crate::task_store::TaskStore>),\n            #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Redis(r) => Some(r.clone() as Arc<dyn crate::task_store::TaskStore>),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Register a new task with metadata (sync, compatible with `TaskRegistry` trait).\n    pub fn register_with_metadata(\n        &self,\n        node_tasks: HashMap<String, u64>,\n        index_uid: Option<String>,\n        task_type: Option<String>,\n    ) -> Result<MiroirTask> {\n        match self {\n            TaskRegistryImpl::InMemory(r) => {\n                crate::task::TaskRegistry::register_with_metadata(\n                    r, node_tasks, index_uid, task_type,\n                )\n            }\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Sqlite(store) => {\n                let miroir_id = format!(\"mtask-{}\", Uuid::new_v4());\n                let created_at = std::time::SystemTime::now()\n                    .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n                    .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Task(format!(\"clock error: {}\", e)))?\n                    .as_millis() as i64;\n\n                let new_task = crate::task_store::NewTask {\n                    miroir_id: miroir_id.clone(),\n                    created_at,\n                    status: \"enqueued\".to_string(),\n                    node_tasks,\n                    error: None,\n                    started_at: None,\n                    finished_at: None,\n                    index_uid,\n                    task_type,\n                    node_errors: HashMap::new(),\n                };\n\n                store.insert_task(&new_task)?;\n\n                Ok(MiroirTask {\n                    miroir_id: new_task.miroir_id,\n                    created_at: new_task.created_at as u64,\n                    started_at: None,\n                    finished_at: None,\n                    status: TaskStatus::Enqueued,\n                    index_uid: new_task.index_uid,\n                    task_type: new_task.task_type,\n                    node_tasks: new_task\n                        .node_tasks\n                        .into_iter()\n                        .map(|(nid, uid)| (nid, NodeTask { task_uid: uid, status: NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued }))\n                        .collect(),\n                    error: None,\n                    node_errors: HashMap::new(),\n                })\n            }\n            #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Redis(store) => {\n                let miroir_id = format!(\"mtask-{}\", Uuid::new_v4());\n                let created_at = std::time::SystemTime::now()\n                    .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n                    .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Task(format!(\"clock error: {}\", e)))?\n                    .as_millis() as i64;\n\n                let new_task = crate::task_store::NewTask {\n                    miroir_id: miroir_id.clone(),\n                    created_at,\n                    status: \"enqueued\".to_string(),\n                    node_tasks,\n                    error: None,\n                    started_at: None,\n                    finished_at: None,\n                    index_uid,\n                    task_type,\n                    node_errors: HashMap::new(),\n                };\n\n                store.insert_task(&new_task)?;\n\n                Ok(MiroirTask {\n                    miroir_id: new_task.miroir_id,\n                    created_at: new_task.created_at as u64,\n                    started_at: None,\n                    finished_at: None,\n                    status: TaskStatus::Enqueued,\n                    index_uid: new_task.index_uid,\n                    task_type: new_task.task_type,\n                    node_tasks: new_task\n                        .node_tasks\n                        .into_iter()\n                        .map(|(nid, uid)| (nid, NodeTask { task_uid: uid, status: NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued }))\n                        .collect(),\n                    error: None,\n                    node_errors: HashMap::new(),\n                })\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get a task by Miroir ID.\n    pub fn get(&self, miroir_id: &str) -> Result<Option<MiroirTask>> {\n        match self {\n            TaskRegistryImpl::InMemory(r) => crate::task::TaskRegistry::get(r, miroir_id),\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Sqlite(store) => {\n                Ok(store.get_task(miroir_id)?.map(row_to_task))\n            }\n            #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Redis(store) => {\n                Ok(store.get_task(miroir_id)?.map(row_to_task))\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Update the overall task status.\n    pub fn update_status(&self, miroir_id: &str, status: TaskStatus) -> Result<()> {\n        match self {\n            TaskRegistryImpl::InMemory(r) => {\n                crate::task::TaskRegistry::update_status(r, miroir_id, status)\n            }\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Sqlite(store) => {\n                store.update_task_status(miroir_id, status_to_str(status))?;\n                Ok(())\n            }\n            #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Redis(store) => {\n                store.update_task_status(miroir_id, status_to_str(status))?;\n                Ok(())\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Update a node task's status.\n    ///\n    /// For persistent backends this is a no-op for per-node status since it is\n    /// reconstructed from polling on each GET /tasks/{id}. The store only persists\n    /// the overall task status and node task UIDs.\n    pub fn update_node_task(\n        &self,\n        miroir_id: &str,\n        node_id: &str,\n        node_status: NodeTaskStatus,\n    ) -> Result<()> {\n        match self {\n            TaskRegistryImpl::InMemory(r) => {\n                crate::task::TaskRegistry::update_node_task(r, miroir_id, node_id, node_status)\n            }\n            // Per-node status is reconstructed from polling on each GET; not persisted.\n            #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Redis(_) => Ok(()),\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Sqlite(_) => Ok(()),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// List tasks with optional filtering.\n    pub fn list(&self, filter: TaskFilter) -> Result<Vec<MiroirTask>> {\n        match self {\n            TaskRegistryImpl::InMemory(r) => crate::task::TaskRegistry::list(r, filter),\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Sqlite(store) => {\n                let store_filter = filter_to_store(&filter);\n                let rows = store.list_tasks(&store_filter)?;\n                Ok(rows.into_iter().map(row_to_task).collect())\n            }\n            #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Redis(store) => {\n                let store_filter = filter_to_store(&filter);\n                let rows = store.list_tasks(&store_filter)?;\n                Ok(rows.into_iter().map(row_to_task).collect())\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Count total tasks.\n    pub fn count(&self) -> usize {\n        match self {\n            TaskRegistryImpl::InMemory(r) => crate::task::TaskRegistry::count(r),\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Sqlite(store) => {\n                store.task_count().unwrap_or(0) as usize\n            }\n            #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Redis(store) => {\n                store.task_count().unwrap_or(0) as usize\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Set error on a task.\n    pub fn set_error(&self, miroir_id: &str, error: &str) -> Result<()> {\n        match self {\n            TaskRegistryImpl::InMemory(_) => Ok(()),\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Sqlite(store) => {\n                store.set_task_error(miroir_id, error)?;\n                Ok(())\n            }\n            #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Redis(store) => {\n                store.set_task_error(miroir_id, error)?;\n                Ok(())\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // --- Alias methods (delegated to TaskStore) ---\n\n    /// Get an alias by name.\n    pub fn get_alias(&self, name: &str) -> Result<Option<crate::alias::Alias>> {\n        match self {\n            TaskRegistryImpl::InMemory(_) => Ok(None),\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Sqlite(store) => {\n                Ok(store.get_alias(name)?.map(|row| crate::alias::Alias {\n                    name: row.name,\n                    kind: if row.kind == \"single\" {\n                        crate::alias::AliasKind::Single\n                    } else {\n                        crate::alias::AliasKind::Multi\n                    },\n                    current_uid: row.current_uid,\n                    target_uids: row.target_uids,\n                    generation: row.version as u64,\n                    created_at: row.created_at as u64,\n                    updated_at: Some(row.created_at as u64), // Use created_at as fallback\n                }))\n            }\n            #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Redis(store) => {\n                Ok(store.get_alias(name)?.map(|row| crate::alias::Alias {\n                    name: row.name,\n                    kind: if row.kind == \"single\" {\n                        crate::alias::AliasKind::Single\n                    } else {\n                        crate::alias::AliasKind::Multi\n                    },\n                    current_uid: row.current_uid,\n                    target_uids: row.target_uids,\n                    generation: row.version as u64,\n                    created_at: row.created_at as u64,\n                    updated_at: Some(row.created_at as u64), // Use created_at as fallback\n                }))\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a new alias.\n    pub fn put_alias(&self, alias: &crate::alias::Alias) -> Result<()> {\n        match self {\n            TaskRegistryImpl::InMemory(_) => Ok(()),\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Sqlite(store) => {\n                let new_alias = crate::task_store::NewAlias {\n                    name: alias.name.clone(),\n                    kind: if matches!(alias.kind, crate::alias::AliasKind::Single) {\n                        \"single\".to_string()\n                    } else {\n                        \"multi\".to_string()\n                    },\n                    current_uid: alias.current_uid.clone(),\n                    target_uids: alias.target_uids.clone(),\n                    version: alias.generation as i64,\n                    created_at: alias.created_at as i64,\n                    history: vec![],\n                };\n                store.create_alias(&new_alias)\n            }\n            #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Redis(store) => {\n                let new_alias = crate::task_store::NewAlias {\n                    name: alias.name.clone(),\n                    kind: if matches!(alias.kind, crate::alias::AliasKind::Single) {\n                        \"single\".to_string()\n                    } else {\n                        \"multi\".to_string()\n                    },\n                    current_uid: alias.current_uid.clone(),\n                    target_uids: alias.target_uids.clone(),\n                    version: alias.generation as i64,\n                    created_at: alias.created_at as i64,\n                    history: vec![],\n                };\n                store.create_alias(&new_alias)\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Delete an alias.\n    pub fn delete_alias(&self, name: &str) -> Result<bool> {\n        match self {\n            TaskRegistryImpl::InMemory(_) => Ok(false),\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Sqlite(store) => store.delete_alias(name),\n            #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Redis(store) => store.delete_alias(name),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// List all aliases.\n    pub fn list_aliases(&self) -> Result<Vec<crate::alias::Alias>> {\n        match self {\n            TaskRegistryImpl::InMemory(_) => Ok(vec![]),\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Sqlite(store) => {\n                let rows = store.list_aliases()?;\n                Ok(rows.into_iter().map(|row| crate::alias::Alias {\n                    name: row.name,\n                    kind: if row.kind == \"single\" {\n                        crate::alias::AliasKind::Single\n                    } else {\n                        crate::alias::AliasKind::Multi\n                    },\n                    current_uid: row.current_uid,\n                    target_uids: row.target_uids,\n                    generation: row.version as u64,\n                    created_at: row.created_at as u64,\n                    updated_at: Some(row.created_at as u64),\n                }).collect())\n            }\n            #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Redis(store) => {\n                let rows = store.list_aliases()?;\n                Ok(rows.into_iter().map(|row| crate::alias::Alias {\n                    name: row.name,\n                    kind: if row.kind == \"single\" {\n                        crate::alias::AliasKind::Single\n                    } else {\n                        crate::alias::AliasKind::Multi\n                    },\n                    current_uid: row.current_uid,\n                    target_uids: row.target_uids,\n                    generation: row.version as u64,\n                    created_at: row.created_at as u64,\n                    updated_at: Some(row.created_at as u64),\n                }).collect())\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[async_trait::async_trait]\nimpl crate::task::TaskRegistry for TaskRegistryImpl {\n    fn register_with_metadata(\n        &self,\n        node_tasks: HashMap<String, u64>,\n        index_uid: Option<String>,\n        task_type: Option<String>,\n    ) -> Result<MiroirTask> {\n        TaskRegistryImpl::register_with_metadata(self, node_tasks, index_uid, task_type)\n    }\n\n    fn get(&self, miroir_id: &str) -> Result<Option<MiroirTask>> {\n        TaskRegistryImpl::get(self, miroir_id)\n    }\n\n    fn update_status(&self, miroir_id: &str, status: TaskStatus) -> Result<()> {\n        TaskRegistryImpl::update_status(self, miroir_id, status)\n    }\n\n    fn update_node_task(\n        &self,\n        miroir_id: &str,\n        node_id: &str,\n        node_status: NodeTaskStatus,\n    ) -> Result<()> {\n        TaskRegistryImpl::update_node_task(self, miroir_id, node_id, node_status)\n    }\n\n    fn list(&self, filter: TaskFilter) -> Result<Vec<MiroirTask>> {\n        TaskRegistryImpl::list(self, filter)\n    }\n\n    fn count(&self) -> usize {\n        TaskRegistryImpl::count(self)\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    #[test]\n    fn test_in_memory_register_creates_task() {\n        let rt = tokio::runtime::Runtime::new().unwrap();\n        let registry = InMemoryTaskRegistry::new();\n        let mut node_tasks = HashMap::new();\n        node_tasks.insert(\"node-0\".to_string(), 1);\n        node_tasks.insert(\"node-1\".to_string(), 2);\n\n        let task = rt.block_on(async {\n            registry.register_async(node_tasks).await\n        }).unwrap();\n        assert!(task.miroir_id.starts_with(\"mtask-\"));\n        assert_eq!(task.status, TaskStatus::Enqueued);\n        assert_eq!(task.node_tasks.len(), 2);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_in_memory_get_returns_task() {\n        let rt = tokio::runtime::Runtime::new().unwrap();\n        let registry = InMemoryTaskRegistry::new();\n        let mut node_tasks = HashMap::new();\n        node_tasks.insert(\"node-0\".to_string(), 1);\n\n        let task = rt.block_on(async {\n            registry.register_async(node_tasks).await\n        }).unwrap();\n        let retrieved = rt.block_on(async {\n            registry.get_async(&task.miroir_id).await\n        });\n        assert!(retrieved.is_some());\n        assert_eq!(retrieved.unwrap().miroir_id, task.miroir_id);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_in_memory_list_filters_by_status() {\n        let rt = tokio::runtime::Runtime::new().unwrap();\n        let registry = InMemoryTaskRegistry::new();\n\n        let mut node_tasks = HashMap::new();\n        node_tasks.insert(\"node-0\".to_string(), 1);\n\n        let (task1, _task2) = rt.block_on(async {\n            let t1 = registry.register_async(node_tasks.clone()).await.unwrap();\n            let t2 = registry.register_async(node_tasks).await.unwrap();\n            (t1, t2)\n        });\n\n        // Update task1 to succeeded - must be done within runtime context\n        let task1_id = task1.miroir_id.clone();\n        rt.block_on(async {\n            let mut tasks = registry.tasks.write().await;\n            if let Some(t) = tasks.get_mut(&task1_id) {\n                t.status = TaskStatus::Succeeded;\n            }\n        });\n\n        let filter = TaskFilter {\n            status: Some(TaskStatus::Succeeded),\n            node_id: None,\n            index_uid: None,\n            task_type: None,\n            limit: None,\n            offset: None,\n        };\n\n        let tasks = rt.block_on(async {\n            registry.list_async(&filter).await\n        }).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(tasks.len(), 1);\n        assert_eq!(tasks[0].miroir_id, task1.miroir_id);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_in_memory_update_node_task() {\n        let rt = tokio::runtime::Runtime::new().unwrap();\n        let registry = InMemoryTaskRegistry::new();\n        let mut node_tasks = HashMap::new();\n        node_tasks.insert(\"node-0\".to_string(), 1);\n\n        let task = rt.block_on(async {\n            registry.register_async(node_tasks).await\n        }).unwrap();\n\n        // Update node task to succeeded - must be done within runtime context\n        let task_id = task.miroir_id.clone();\n        rt.block_on(async {\n            let mut tasks = registry.tasks.write().await;\n            if let Some(t) = tasks.get_mut(&task_id) {\n                if let Some(nt) = t.node_tasks.get_mut(\"node-0\") {\n                    nt.status = NodeTaskStatus::Succeeded;\n                }\n            }\n        });\n\n        let retrieved = rt.block_on(async {\n            registry.get_async(&task.miroir_id).await\n        }).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(retrieved.node_tasks.get(\"node-0\").unwrap().status, NodeTaskStatus::Succeeded);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_update_overall_status() {\n        let rt = tokio::runtime::Runtime::new().unwrap();\n        let registry = InMemoryTaskRegistry::new();\n        let mut node_tasks = HashMap::new();\n        node_tasks.insert(\"node-0\".to_string(), 1);\n        node_tasks.insert(\"node-1\".to_string(), 2);\n\n        let task = rt.block_on(async {\n            registry.register_async(node_tasks).await\n        }).unwrap();\n\n        // Mark one node as succeeded, one as processing - must be done within runtime context\n        let task_id = task.miroir_id.clone();\n        rt.block_on(async {\n            let mut tasks = registry.tasks.write().await;\n            if let Some(t) = tasks.get_mut(&task_id) {\n                if let Some(nt) = t.node_tasks.get_mut(\"node-0\") {\n                    nt.status = preflight responses into global IDF.\n67\t    pub fn from_preflight_responses(responses: &[PreflightResponse]) -> Self {\n68\t        let mut total_docs = 0u64;\n69\t        let mut total_length = 0.0f64;\n70\t        let mut term_df: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();\n71\t\n72\t        for resp in responses {\n73\t            total_docs += resp.total_docs;\n74\t            total_length += resp.avg_doc_length * resp.total_docs as f64;\n75\t            for (term, stats) in &resp.term_stats {\n76\t                *term_df.entry(term.clone()).or_insert(0) += stats.df;\n77\t            }\n78\t        }\n79\t\n80\t        let avg_doc_length = if total_docs > 0 {\n81\t            total_length / total_docs as f64\n82\t        } else {\n83\t            0.0\n84\t        };\n85\t\n86\t        let n = total_docs as f64;\n87\t        let terms = term_df\n88\t            .into_iter()\n89\t            .map(|(term, df)| {\n90\t                let idf = if df == 0 {\n91\t                    0.0\n92\t                } else {\n93\t                    ((n - df as f64 + 0.5) / (df as f64 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0\n94\t                };\n95\t                (term, GlobalTermStats { df, idf })\n96\t            })\n97\t            .collect();\n98\t\n99\t        Self { total_docs, avg_doc_length, terms }\n100\t    }\n101\t}\n102\t\n103\t// Meilisearch status string to NodeTaskStatus.\n203\t    pub fn to_node_status(&self) -> crate::task::NodeTaskStatus {\n204\t        match self.status.as_str() {\n205\t            \"enqueued\" => crate::task::NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued,\n206\t            \"processing\" => crate::task::NodeTaskStatus::Processing,\n207\t            \"succeeded\" => crate::task::NodeTaskStatus::Succeeded,\n208\t            \"failed\" => crate::task::NodeTaskStatus::Failed,\n209\t            _ => crate::task::NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued,\n210\t        }\n211\t    }\n212\t}\n213\t\n214\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n215\t// NodeClient trait\n216\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n217\t\n218\t/// HTTP client for communicating with a Meilisearch node.\n219\t#[allow(async_fn_in_trait)]\n220\tpub trait NodeClient: Send + Sync {\n221\t    async fn search_node(\n222\t        &self,\n223\t        node: &NodeId,\n224\t        address: &str,\n225\t        request: &SearchRequest,\n226\t    ) -> std::result::Result<Value, NodeError>;\n227\t\n228\t    /// Execute a preflight request (OP#4 global-IDF phase).\n229\t    async fn preflight_node(\n230\t        &self,\n231\t        _node: &NodeId,\n232\t        _address: &str,\n233\t        _request: &PreflightRequest,\n234\t    ) -> std::result::Result<PreflightResponse, NodeError> {\n235\t        Ok(PreflightResponse { total_docs: 0, avg_doc_length: 0.0, term_stats: HashMap::new() })\n236\t    }\n237\t\n238\t    /// Write documents to a node (add/replace).\n239\t    async fn write_documents(\n240\t        &self,\n241\t        _node: &NodeId,\n242\t        _address: &str,\n243\t        _request: &WriteRequest,\n244\t    ) -> std::result::Result<WriteResponse, NodeError> {\n245\t        Ok(WriteResponse {\n246\t            success: false,\n247\t            task_uid: None,\n248\t            message: Some(\"not implemented\".to_string()),\n249\t            code: None,\n250\t            error_type: None,\n251\t        })\n252\t    }\n253\t\n254\t    /// Get task status from a node.\n255\t    fn get_task_status(\n256\t        &self,\n257\t        _node: &NodeId,\n258\t        _address: &str,\n259\t        _request: &TaskStatusRequest,\n260\t    ) -> impl std::future::Future<Output = std::result::Result<TaskStatusResponse, NodeError>> + Send {\n261\t        async move {\n262\t            Ok(TaskStatusResponse {\n263\t                task_uid: _request.task_uid,\n264\t                status: \"succeeded\".to_string(),\n265\t                error: None,\n266\t                error_type: None,\n267\t            })\n268\t        }\n269\t    }\n270\t\n271\t    /// Delete documents by IDs from a node.\n272\t    async fn delete_documents(\n273\t        &self,\n274\t        _node: &NodeId,\n275\t        _address: &str,\n276\t        _request: &DeleteByIdsRequest,\n277\t    ) -> std::result::Result<DeleteResponse, NodeError> {\n278\t        Ok(DeleteResponse {\n279\t            success: false,\n280\t            task_uid: None,\n281\t            message: Some(\"not implemented\".to_string()),\n282\t            code: None,\n283\t            error_type: None,\n284\t        })\n285\t    }\n286\t\n287\t    /// Delete all documents matching a filter from a node.\n288\t    async fn delete_documents_by_filter(\n289\t        &self,\n290\t        _node: &NodeId,\n291\t        _address: &str,\n292\t        _request: &DeleteByFilterRequest,\n293\t    ) -> std::result::Result<DeleteResponse, NodeError> {\n294\t        Ok(DeleteResponse {\n295\t            success: false,\n296\t            task_uid: documents with a filter from a node (used for shard migration).\n304\t    async fn fetch_documents(\n305\t        &self,\n306\t        _node: &NodeId,\n307\t        _address: &str,\n308\t        _request: &FetchDocumentsRequest,\n309\t    ) -> std::result::Result<FetchDocumentsResponse, NodeError> {\n310\t        Ok(FetchDocumentsResponse {\n311\t            results: Vec::new(),\n312\t            limit: _request.limit,\n313\t            offset: _request.offset,\n314\t            total: 0,\n315\t        })\n316\t    }\n317\t}\n318\t\n319\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n320\tpub enum NodeError {\n321\t    Timeout,\n322\t    HttpError { status: u16, body: String },\n323\t    NetworkError(String),\n324\t}\n325\t\n326\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n327\tpub struct SearchRequest {\n328\t    pub index_uid: String,\n329\t    pub query: Option<String>,\n330\t    pub offset: usize,\n331\t    pub limit: usize,\n332\t    pub filter: Option<Value>,\n333\t    pub facets: Option<Vec<String>>,\n334\t    pub ranking_score: bool,\n335\t    pub body: Value,\n336\t    /// Global IDF data from the preflight phase (OP#4).\n337\t    pub global_idf: Option<GlobalIdf>,\n338\t}\n339\t\n340\timpl SearchRequest {\n341\t    /// Build the request body for sending to a node.\n342\t    ///\n343\t    /// Injects `showRankingScore: true` unconditionally so the merger can global-sort.\n344\t    /// Each node receives `offset + limit` results to ensure the coordinator has enough\n345\t    /// data to apply pagination.\n346\t    pub fn to_node_body(&self) -> Value {\n347\t        let mut body = self.body.clone();\n348\t\n349\t        // Inject showRankingScore: true unconditionally for global sorting\n350\t        body[\"showRankingScore\"] = serde_json::json!(true);\n351\t\n352\t        // Set limit to offset + limit so we get enough results for pagination\n353\t        // (coordinator applies final offset/limit after merging)\n354\t        body[\"limit\"] = serde_json::json!(self.offset + self.limit);\n355\t\n356\t        // Set offset to 0 on individual nodes (coordinator handles offset)\n357\t        body[\"offset\"] = serde_json::json!(0);\n358\t\n359\t        // Ensure query is set\n360\t        if let Some(q) = &self.query {\n361\t            body[\"q\"] = serde_json::json!(q);\n362\t        }\n363\t\n364\t        // Ensure filter is set if provided\n365\t        if let Some(filter) = &self.filter {\n366\t            body[\"filter\"] = filter.clone();\n367\t        }\n368\t\n369\t        // Ensure facets are set if provided\n370\t        if let Some(facets) = &self.facets {\n371\t            body[\"facets\"] = serde_json::json!(facets);\n372\t        }\n373\t\n374\t        body\n375\t    }\n376\t}\n377\t\n378\t#[derive(Debug)]\n379\tpub struct ScatterResult {\n380\t    pub shard_pages: Vec<ShardHitPage>,\n381\t    pub failed_shards: HashMap<u32, NodeError>,\n382\t    pub partial: bool,\n383\t    pub deadline_exceeded: bool,\n384\t}\n385\t\n386\t#[instrument(skip_all, fields(query_seq, rf, shard_count))]\n387\tpub async fn plan_search_scatter(\n388\t    topology: &Topology,\n389\t    query_seq: u64,\n390\t    rf: usize,\n391\t    shard_count: u32,\n392\t    replica_selector: Option<&ReplicaSelector>,\n393\t) -> ScatterPlan {\n394\t    let chosen_group = query_group(query_seq, topology.replica_group_count());\n395\t\n396\t    let group = match topology.group(chosen_group) {\n397\t        Some(g) => g,\n398\t        None => {\n399\t            return ScatterPlan {\n400\t                chosen_group, target_shards: Vec::new(),\n401\t                shard_to_node: HashMap::new(), deadline_ms: 0, hedging_eligible: false,\n402\t            };\n403\t        }\n404\t    };\n405\t\n406\t    let _covering = covering_set(shard_count, group, rf, query_seq);\n407\t\n408\t    let mut shard_to_node = HashMap::new();\n409\t    for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n410\t        let replicas = crate::router::assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), rf);\n411\t\n412\t        let selected = if let Some(selector) = replica_selector {\n413\t            match selector.select(&replicas, chosen_group).await {\n414\t                Some(node) => node,\n415\t                None => replicas[(query_seq as usize) % replicas.len()].clone(),\n416\t            }\n417\t        } else {\n418\t            replicas[(query_seq as usize) % replicas.len()].clone()\n419\t        };\n420\t\n421\t        shard_to_node.insert(shard_id, selected);\n422\t    }\n423\t\n424\t    ScatterPlan {\n425\t        chosen_group,\n426\t        target_shards: (0..shard_count).collect(),\n427\t        shard_to_node,\n428\t        deadline_ms: 5000,\n429\t        hedging_eligible: group.node_count() > 1,\n430\t    }\n431\t}\n432\t\n433\t/// Plan search scatter with settings version floor filtering (plan §13.5).\n434\t///\n435\t/// Excludes nodes whose settings version for the given index is below `floor`.\n436\t/// Returns None if no covering set can be assembled (caller should return 503).\n437\tpub async fn plan_search_scatter_with_version_floor(\n438\t    topology: &Topology,\n439\t    query_seq: u64,\n440\t    rf: usize,\n441\t    shard_count: u32,\n442\t    index: &str,\n443\t    floor: u64,\n444\t    version_checker: &impl Fn(&str, &str) -> u64,\n445\t    replica_selector: Option<&ReplicaSelector>,\n446\t) -> Option<ScatterPlan> {\n447\t    let chosen_group = query_group(query_seq, topology.replica_group_count());\n448\t\n449\t    let group = topology.group(chosen_group)?;\n450\t\n451\t    let covering = covering_set_with_version_floor(\n452\t        shard_count,\n453\t        group,\n454\t        rf,\n455\t        query_seq,\n456\t        index,\n457\t        floor,\n458\t        version_checker,\n459\t    )?;\n460\t\n461\t    let mut shard_to_node = HashMap::new();\n462\t    for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n463\t        let replicas = crate::router::assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), rf);\n464\t        // Filter by version floor\n465\t        let eligible: Vec<_> = replicas\n466\t            .iter()\n467\t            .filter(|node_id| {\n468\t                let version = version_checker(index, node_id.as_str());\n469\t                version >= floor\n470\t            })\n471\t            .collect();\n472\t\n473\t        if eligible.is_empty() {\n474\t            return None;\n475\t        }\n476\t\n477\t        let selected = if let Some(selector) = replica_selector {\n478\t            // Convert Vec<&NodeId> to Vec<NodeId> for selector\n479\t            let eligible_owned: Vec<NodeId> = eligible.iter().map(|&n| n.clone()).collect();\n480\t            match selector.select(&eligible_owned, chosen_group).await {\n481\t                Some(node) => node,\n482\t                None => eligible[query_seq as usize % eligible.len()].clone(),\n483\t            }\n484\t        } else {\n485\t            eligible[query_seq as usize % eligible.len()].clone()\n486\t        };\n487\t\n488\t        shard_to_node.insert(shard_id, selected.clone());\n489\t    }\n490\t\n491\t    Some(ScatterPlan {\n492\t        chosen_group,\n493\t        target_shards: (0..shard_count).collect(),\n494\t        shard_to_node,\n495\t        deadline_ms: 5000,\n496\t        hedging_eligible: group.node_count() > 1,\n497\t    })\n498\t}\n499\t\n500\t/// Plan search scatter for a specific replica group (plan §13.6 session pinning).\n501\t///\n502\t/// Used when a session has a pending write and needs to read from the pinned group\n503\t/// to ensure read-your-writes consistency.\n504\tpub async fn plan_search_scatter_for_group(\n505\t    topology: &Topology,\n506\t    query_seq: u64,\n507\t    rf: usize,\n508\t    shard_count: u32,\n509\t    pinned_group: u32,\n510\t    replica_selector: Option<&ReplicaSelector>,\n511\t) -> Option<ScatterPlan> {\n512\t    let group = topology.group(pinned_group)?;\n513\t\n514\t    let mut shard_to_node = HashMap::new();\n515\t    for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n516\t        let replicas = crate::router::assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), rf);\n517\t        if replicas.is_empty() {\n518\t            continue;\n519\t        }\n520\t\n521\t        let selected = if let Some(selector) = replica_selector {\n522\t            match selector.select(&replicas, pinned_group).await {\n523\t                Some(node) => node,\n524\t                None => replicas[query_seq as usize % replicas.len()].clone(),\n525\t            }\n526\t        } else {\n527\t            replicas[query_seq as usize % replicas.len()].clone()\n528\t        };\n529\t\n530\t        shard_to_node.insert(shard_id, selected);\n531\t    }\n532\t\n533\t    Some(ScatterPlan {\n534\t        chosen_group: pinned_group,\n535\t        target_shards: (0..shard_count).collect(),\n536\t        shard_to_node,\n537\t        deadline_ms: 5000,\n538\t        hedging_eligible: group.node_count() > 1,\n539\t    })\n540\t}\n541\t\n542\t/// Plan search scatter using adaptive replica selection (plan §13.3).\n543\t///\n544\t/// Uses EWMA-based scoring to select the best replica for each shard,\n545\t/// falling back to round-robin for shards with no metrics data.\n546\t#[instrument(skip_all, fields(query_seq, rf, shard_count))]\n547\tpub async fn plan_search_scatter_adaptive(\n548\t    topology: &Topology,\n549\t    query_seq: u64,\n550\t    rf: usize,\n551\t    shard_count: u32,\n552\t    replica_selector: &ReplicaSelector,\n553\t) -> ScatterPlan {\n554\t    let chosen_group = query_group(query_seq, topology.replica_group_count());\n555\t\n556\t    let group = match topology.group(chosen_group) {\n557\t        Some(g) => g,\n558\t        None => {\n559\t            return ScatterPlan {\n560\t                chosen_group,\n561\t                target_shards: Vec::new(),\n562\t                shard_to_node: HashMap::new(),\n563\t                deadline_ms: 0,\n564\t                hedging_eligible: false,\n565\t            };\n566\t        }\n567\t    };\n568\t\n569\t    // If the group has no nodes, return a plan with no targets\n570\t    if group.nodes().is_empty() {\n571\t        return ScatterPlan {\n572\t            chosen_group,\n573\t            target_shards: Vec::new(),\n574\t            shard_to_node: HashMap::new(),\n575\t            deadline_ms: 5000,\n576\t            hedging_eligible: false,\n577\t        };\n578\t    }\n579\t\n580\t    let mut shard_to_node = HashMap::new();\n581\t    for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n582\t        let replicas = crate::router::assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), rf);\n583\t        if replicas.is_empty() {\n584\t            continue;\n585\t        }\n586\t\n587\t        // Use adaptive selection to pick the best replica\n588\t        let selected = match replica_selector.select(&replicas, chosen_group).await {\n589\t            Some(node) => node,\n590\t            None => {\n591\t                // Fallback to round-robin if selector returns None\n592\t                replicas[(query_seq as usize) % replicas.len()].clone()\n593\t            }\n594\t        };\n595\t\n596\t        shard_to_node.insert(shard_id, selected);\n597\t    }\n598\t\n599\t    ScatterPlan {\n600\t        chosen_group,\n601\t        target_shards: (0..shard_count).collect(),\n602\t        shard_to_node,\n603\t        deadline_ms: 5000,\n604\t        hedging_eligible: group.node_count() > 1,\n605\t    }\n606\t}\n607\t\n608\t#[instrument(skip_all, fields(node_count))]\n609\tpub async fn execute_scatter<C: NodeClient>(\n610\t    plan: ScatterPlan,\n611\t    client: &C,\n612\t    req: SearchRequest,\n613\t    topology: &Topology,\n614\t    policy: UnavailableShardPolicy,\n615\t) -> Result<ScatterResult> {\n616\t    let mut node_to_shards: HashMap<NodeId, Vec<u32>> = HashMap::new();\n617\t    for (&shard_id, node_id) in &plan.shard_to_node {\n618\t        if plan.target_shards.contains(&shard_id) {\n619\t            node_to_shards.entry(node_id.clone()).or_default().push(shard_id);\n620\t        }\n621\t    }\n622\t    tracing::Span::current().record(\"node_count\", node_to_shards.len());\n623\t\n624\t    let mut shard_pages = Vec::new();\n625\t    let mut failed_shards = HashMap::new();\n626\t    let mut deadline_exceeded = false;\n627\t\n628\t    let mut tasks = Vec::new();\n629\t    for (node_id, shards) in node_to_shards {\n630\t        let node = match topology.node(&node_id) {\n631\t            Some(n) => n.clone(),\n632\t            None => {\n633\t                for shard_id in shards {\n634\t                    failed_shards.insert(shard_id, NodeError::NetworkError(\"node not in topology\".to_string()));\n635\t                }\n636\t                continue;\n637\t            }\n638\t        };\n639\t        let client_ref = client;\n640\t        let req_clone = req.clone();\n641\t        let node_id_clone = node_id.clone();\n642\t        let shard_count = shards.len();\n643\t        // Create a span for this node's scatter call\n644\t        let span = info_span!(\n645\t            \"scatter_node\",\n646\t            node_id = %node_id_clone,\n647\t            address = %node.address,\n648\t            shard_count = shard_count,\n649\t        );\n650\t        tasks.push(async move {\n651\t            let result = client_ref.search_node(&node_id_clone, &node.address, &req_clone).await;\n652\t            (node_id_clone, shards, result)\n653\t        }.instrument(span));\n654\t    }\n655\t\n656\t    let results = futures_util::future::join_all(tasks).await;\n657\t\n658\t    for (_node_id, shards, result) in results {\n659\t        match result {\n660\t            Ok(body) => {\n661\t                for _shard_id in shards {\n662\t                    shard_pages.push(ShardHitPage { body: body.clone() });\n663\t                }\n664\t            }\n665\t            Err(NodeError::Timeout) => {\n666\t                deadline_exceeded = true;\n667\t                for shard_id in shards { failed_shards.insert(shard_id, NodeError::Timeout); }\n668\t            }\n669\t            Err(e) => {\n670\t                for shard_id in shards { failed_shards.insert(shard_id, e.clone()); }\n671\t            }\n672\t        }\n673\t    }\n674\t\n675\t    let partial = !failed_shards.is_empty();\n676\t\n677\t    match policy {\n678\t        UnavailableShardPolicy::Error => {\n679\t            if !failed_shards.is_empty() {\n680\t                return Err(crate::error::MiroirError::Routing(format!(\"{} shard(s) unavailable\", failed_shards.len())));\n681\t            }\n682\t        }\n683\t        UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial => {}\n684\t        UnavailableShardPolicy::Fallback => {\n685\t            // Group-unavailability fallback: try other replica groups for failed shards\n686\t            if !failed_shards.is_empty() {\n687\t                let mut fallback_pages = Vec::new();\n688\t                let mut remaining_failed = HashMap::new();\n689\t\n690\t                for (&shard_id, error) in &failed_shards {\n691\t                    let mut fallback_succeeded = false;\n692\t\n693\t                    // Try each other replica group\n694\t                    for group_id in 0..topology.replica_group_count() {\n695\t                        if group_id == plan.chosen_group {\n696\t                            continue; // Skip the already-tried group\n697\t                        }\n698\t\n699\t                        if let Some(group) = topology.group(group_id) {\n700\t                            let replicas = crate::router::assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), topology.rf());\n701\t                            if replicas.is_empty() {\n702\t                                continue;\n703\t                            }\n704\t\n705\t                            // Try each replica in the fallback group\n706\t                            for node_id in replicas {\n707\t                                if let Some(node) = topology.node(&node_id) {\n708\t                                    match client.search_node(&node_id, &node.address, &req).await {\n709\t                                        Ok(body) => {\n710\t                                            fallback_pages.push(ShardHitPage { body });\n711\t                                            fallback_succeeded = true;\n712\t                                            break;\n713\t                                        }\n714\t                                        Err(_) => {\n715\t                                            continue; // Try next replica\n716\t                                        }\n717\t                                    }\n718\t                                }\n719\t                            }\n720\t\n721\t                            if fallback_succeeded {\n722\t                                break; // Found a working replica\n723\t                            }\n724\t                        }\n725\t                    }\n726\t\n727\t                    if !fallback_succeeded {\n728\t                        remaining_failed.insert(shard_id, error.clone());\n729\t                    }\n730\t                }\n731\t\n732\t                // Merge fallback results\n733\t                shard_pages.extend(fallback_pages);\n734\t\n735\t                // Update failed_shards with only those that truly failed\n736\t                if remaining_failed.is_empty() {\n737\t                    // All shards succeeded via fallback\n738\t                    return Ok(ScatterResult {\n739\t                        shard_pages,\n740\t                        failed_shards: HashMap::new(),\n741\t                        partial: false,\n742\t                        deadline_exceeded,\n743\t                    });\n744\t                } else {\n745\t                    // Some shards still failed\n746\t                    failed_shards = remaining_failed;\n747\t                }\n748\t            }\n749\t        }\n750\t    }\n751\t\n752\t    Ok(ScatterResult { shard_pages, failed_shards, partial, deadline_exceeded })\n753\t}\n754\t\n755\t#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %req.index_uid))]\n756\tpub async fn scatter_gather_search<C: NodeClient>(\n757\t    plan: ScatterPlan,\n758\t    client: &C,\n759\t    req: SearchRequest,\n760\t    topology: &Topology,\n761\t    policy: UnavailableShardPolicy,\n762\t    strategy: &dyn MergeStrategy,\n763\t) -> Result<MergedSearchResult> {\n764\t    let scatter_result = execute_scatter(plan, client, req.clone(), topology, policy).await?;\n765\t\n766\t    let mut shard_pages = scatter_result.shard_pages;\n767\t    if scatter_result.partial {\n768\t        for shard_id in scatter_result.failed_shards.keys() {\n769\t            shard_pages.push(ShardHitPage {\n770\t                body: serde_json::json!({\"success\": false, \"message\": format!(\"shard {} unavailable\", shard_id)}),\n771\t            });\n772\t        }\n773\t    }\n774\t\n775\t    // Collect failed shard IDs for the X-Miroir-Degraded header\n776\t    let failed_shards: Vec<u32> = scatter_result.failed_shards.keys().copied().collect();\n777\t\n778\t    let merge_input = MergeInput {\n779\t        shard_hits: shard_pages,\n780\t        offset: req.offset,\n781\t        limit: req.limit,\n782\t        client_requested_score: req.ranking_score,\n783\t        facets: req.facets.clone(),\n784\t        failed_shards,\n785\t    };\n786\t\n787\t    // Span for the merge operation\n788\t    let _span = info_span!(\n789\t        \"merge\",\n790\t        shard_count = merge_input.shard_hits.len(),\n791\t        offset = req.offset,\n792\t        limit = req.limit,\n793\t    ).entered();\n794\t\n795\t    strategy.merge(merge_input)\n796\t}\n797\t\n798\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n799\t// OP#4: Global-IDF preflight execution\n800\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n801\t\n802\t/// Extract unique query terms from a search query string.\n803\tpub fn extract_query_terms(query: &Option<String>) -> Vec<String> {\n804\t    match query {\n805\t        Some(q) if !q.is_empty() => {\n806\t            let mut seen = std::collections::HashSet::new();\n807\t            let mut terms = Vec::new();\n808\t            for term in q.split_whitespace() {\n809\t                let lower = term.to_lowercase();\n810\t                if seen.insert(lower.clone()) { terms.push(lower); }\n811\t            }\n812\t            terms\n813\t        }\n814\t        _ => Vec::new(),\n815\t    }\n816\t}\n817\t\n818\t/// Execute the preflight phase: gather term frequencies from all shards.\n819\t#[instrument(skip_all, fields(node_count, term_count = req.terms.len()))]\n820\tpub async fn execute_preflight<C: NodeClient>(\n821\t    plan: &ScatterPlan,\n822\t    client: &C,\n823\t    req: &PreflightRequest,\n824\t    topology: &Topology,\n825\t) -> Result<GlobalIdf> {\n826\t    if req.terms.is_empty() {\n827\t        return Ok(GlobalIdf { total_docs: 0, avg_doc_length: 0.0, terms: HashMap::new() });\n828\t    }\n829\t\n830\t    let mut node_to_shards: HashMap<NodeId, Vec<u32>> = HashMap::new();\n831\t    for (&shard_id, node_id) in &plan.shard_to_node {\n832\t        if plan.target_shards.contains(&shard_id) {\n833\t            node_to_shards.entry(node_id.clone()).or_default().push(shard_id);\n834\t        }\n835\t    }\n836\t\n837\t    let mut tasks = Vec::new();\n838\t    for (node_id, _) in node_to_shards {\n839\t        let node = match topology.node(&node_id) {\n840\t            Some(n) => n.clone(),\n841\t            None => continue,\n842\t        };\n843\t        let client_ref = client;\n844\t        let req_clone = req.clone();\n845\t        let nid = node_id.clone();\n846\t        tasks.push(async move { client_ref.preflight_node(&nid, &node.address, &req_clone).await });\n847\t    }\n848\t\n849\t    let results = futures_util::future::join_all(tasks).await;\n850\t    let responses: Vec<PreflightResponse> = results.into_iter().filter_map(|r| r.ok()).collect();\n851\t    Ok(GlobalIdf::from_preflight_responses(&responses))\n852\t}\n853\t\n854\t/// Execute a full dfs_query_then_fetch search (OP#4 global-IDF preflight).\n855\t#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %req.index_uid))]\n856\tpub async fn dfs_query_then_fetch_search<C: NodeClient>(\n857\t    plan: ScatterPlan,\n858\t    client: &C,\n859\t    req: SearchRequest,\n860\t    topology: &Topology,\n861\t    policy: UnavailableShardPolicy,\n862\t    strategy: &dyn MergeStrategy,\n863\t) -> Result<MergedSearchResult> {\n864\t    let preflight_req = PreflightRequest {\n865\t        index_uid: req.index_uid.clone(),\n866\t        terms: extract_query_terms(&req.query),\n867\t        filter: req.filter.clone(),\n868\t    };\n869\t    let global_idf = execute_preflight(&plan, client, &preflight_req, topology).await?;\n870\t    let mut search_req = req;\n871\t    search_req.global_idf = Some(global_idf);\n872\t    scatter_gather_search(plan, client, search_req, topology, policy, strategy).await\n873\t}\n874\t\n875\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n876\t// Mock client\n877\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n878\t\n879\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\n880\tpub struct MockNodeClient {\n881\t    pub responses: HashMap<NodeId, Value>,\n882\t    pub preflight_responses: HashMap<NodeId, PreflightResponse>,\n883\t    pub write_responses: HashMap<NodeId, WriteResponse>,\n884\t    pub fetch_responses: HashMap<NodeId, FetchDocumentsResponse>,\n885\t    pub errors: HashMap<NodeId, NodeError>,\n886\t    pub delay_ms: u64,\n887\t}\n888\t\n889\timpl NodeClient for MockNodeClient {\n890\t    async fn search_node(\n891\t        &self, node: &NodeId, _address: &str, _request: &SearchRequest,\n892\t    ) -> std::result::Result<Value, NodeError> {\n893\t        let _ = self.delay_ms;\n894\t        if let Some(err) = self.errors.get(node) { return Err(err.clone()); }\n895\t        Ok(self.responses.get(node).cloned().unwrap_or_else(|| {\n896\t            serde_json::json!({\"hits\": [], \"estimatedTotalHits\": 0, \"processingTimeMs\": 0, \"facetDistribution\": {}})\n897\t        }))\n898\t    }\n899\t\n900\t    async fn preflight_node(\n901\t        &self, node: &NodeId, _address: &str, _request: &PreflightRequest,\n902\t    ) -> std::result::Result<PreflightResponse, NodeError> {\n903\t        if let Some(err) = self.errors.get(node) { return Err(err.clone()); }\n904\t        Ok(self.preflight_responses.get(node).cloned().unwrap_or_else(|| {\n905\t            PreflightResponse { total_docs: 1000, avg_doc_length: 50.0, term_stats: HashMap::new() }\n906\t        }))\n907\t    }\n908\t\n909\t    async fn write_documents(\n910\t        &self, node: &NodeId, _address: &str, _request: &WriteRequest,\n911\t    ) -> std::result::Result<WriteResponse, NodeError> {\n912\t        if let Some(err) = self.errors.get(node) { return Err(err.clone()); }\n913\t        Ok(self.write_responses.get(node).cloned().unwrap_or_else(|| {\n914\t            WriteResponse {\n915\t                success: true,\n916\t                task_uid: Some(1),\n917\t                message: None,\n918\t                code: None,\n919\t                error_type: None,\n920\t            }\n921\t        }))\n922\t    }\n923\t\n924\t    async fn delete_documents(\n925\t        &self, node: &NodeId, _address: &str, _request: &DeleteByIdsRequest,\n926\t    ) -> std::result::Result<DeleteResponse, NodeError> {\n927\t        if let Some(err) = self.errors.get(node) { return Err(err.clone()); }\n928\t        Ok(DeleteResponse {\n929\t            success: true,\n930\t            task_uid: Some(1),\n931\t            message: None,\n932\t            code: None,\n933\t            error_type: None,\n934\t        })\n935\t    }\n936\t\n937\t    async fn delete_documents_by_filter(\n938\t        &self, node: &NodeId, _address: &str, _request: &DeleteByFilterRequest,\n939\t    ) -> std::result::Result<DeleteResponse, NodeError> {\n940\t        if let Some(err) = self.errors.get(node) { return Err(err.clone()); }\n941\t        Ok(DeleteResponse {\n942\t            success: true,\n943\t            task_uid: Some(1),\n944\t            message: None,\n945\t            code: None,\n946\t            error_type: None,\n947\t        })\n948\t    }\n949\t\n950\t    async fn fetch_documents(\n951\t        &self, node: &NodeId, _address: &str, request: &FetchDocumentsRequest,\n952\t    ) -> std::result::Result<FetchDocumentsResponse, NodeError> {\n953\t        if let Some(err) = self.errors.get(node) { return Err(err.clone()); }\n954\t        // Return stored fetch response if available, otherwise return empty\n955\t        let stored = self.fetch_responses.get(node).cloned().unwrap_or_else(|| {\n956\t            FetchDocumentsResponse {\n957\t                results: Vec::new(),\n958\t                limit: request.limit,\n959\t                offset: request.offset,\n960\t                total: 0,\n961\t            }\n962\t        });\n963\t\n964\t        // Handle pagination: if offset exceeds total, return empty results\n965\t        if request.offset as u64 >= stored.total {\n966\t            return Ok(FetchDocumentsResponse {\n967\t                results: Vec::new(),\n968\t                limit: request.limit,\n969\t                offset: request.offset,\n970\t                total: stored.total,\n971\t            });\n972\t        }\n973\t\n974\t        Ok(stored)\n975\t    }\n976\t\n977\t    fn get_task_status(\n978\t        &self,\n979\t        node: &NodeId,\n980\t        _address: &str,\n981\t        _request: &TaskStatusRequest,\n982\t    ) -> impl std::future::Future<Output = std::result::Result<TaskStatusResponse, NodeError>> + Send {\n983\t        let node = node.clone();\n984\t        let task_uid = _request.task_uid;\n985\t        let error = self.errors.get(&node).cloned();\n986\t        async move {\n987\t            if let Some(err) = error {\n988\t                return Err(err);\n989\t            }\n990\t            Ok(TaskStatusResponse {\n991\t                task_uid,\n992\t                status: \"succeeded\".to_string(),\n993\t                error: None,\n994\t                error_type: None,\n995\t            })\n996\t        }\n997\t    }\n998\t}\n999\t\n1000\t#[cfg(test)]\n1001\tmod tests {\n1002\t    use super::*;\n1003\t    use crate::topology::{Node, {\n1177\t            total_docs: 50000, avg_doc_length: 50.0,\n1178\t            term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"test\".into(), TermStats { df: 500 })]),\n1179\t        });\n1180\t        let s = assert!(!r.degraded);\n1183\t        assert!(!r.hits.is_empty());\n1184\t    }\n1185\t\n1186\t    /// Integration test for dfs_query_then_fetch with severely skewed shard distribution.\n1187\t    ///\n1188\t    /// This test simulates the scenario described in miroir-yio:\n1189\t    /// - Shard 0: Normal (1,000 docs, term df = 100)\n1190\t    /// - Shard 1: 100x normal (100,000 docs, term df = 10,000)\n1191\t    /// - Shard 2: Near-empty (10 docs, term df = 1)\n1192\t    ///\n1193\t    /// Without global IDF preflight, each shard would compute different local IDF values:\n1194\t    /// - Shard 0: IDF ≈ log((1000 - 100 + 0.5) / (100 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.3\n1195\t    /// - Shard 1: IDF ≈ log((100000 - 10000 + 0.5) / (10000 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.3\n1196\t    /// - Shard 2: IDF ≈ log((10 - 1 + 0.5) / (1 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.8\n1197\t    ///\n1198\t    /// With global IDF preflight, all shards use the same IDF:\n1199\t    /// - Global: N = 101,010, df = 10,101\n1200\t    /// - IDF ≈ log((101010 - 10101 + 0.5) / (10101 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.3\n1201\t    ///\n1202\t    /// This ensures scores are comparable across shards, enabling correct score-based merge.\n1203\t    #[tokio::test]\n1204\t    async fn test_dfs_skewed_shards_global_idf_aggregation() {\n1205\t        let mut topo = Topology::new(3, 1, 1);\n1206\t        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), \"http://node-0:7700\".into(), 0));\n1207\t        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), \"http://node-1:7700\".into(), 0));\n1208\t        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()), \"http://node-2:7700\".into(), 0));\n1209\t\n1210\t        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 1, 3, None).await;\n1211\t\n1212\t        // Simulate severely skewed shard distribution\n1213\t        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n1214\t\n1215\t        // Shard 0: Normal distribution\n1216\t        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n1217\t            total_docs: 1000,\n1218\t            avg_doc_length: 50.0,\n1219\t            term_stats: HashMap::from([\n1220\t                (\"machine\".into(), TermStats { df: 100 }),\n1221\t                (\"learning\".into(), TermStats { df: 80 }),\n1222\t            ]),\n1223\t        });\n1224\t\n1225\t        // Shard 1: 100x normal (severely skewed)\n1226\t        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n1227\t            total_docs: 100_000,\n1228\t            avg_doc_length: 55.0,\n1229\t            term_stats: HashMap::from([\n1230\t                (\"machine\".into(), TermStats { df: 10_000 }),\n1231\t                (\"learning\".into(), TermStats { df: 8_000 }),\n1232\t            ]),\n1233\t        });\n1234\t\n1235\t        // Shard 2: Near-empty\n1236\t        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n1237\t            total_docs: 10,\n1238\t            avg_doc_length: 45.0,\n1239\t            term_stats: HashMap::from([\n1240\t                (\"machine\".into(), TermStats { df: 1 }),\n1241\t                (\"learning\".into(), TermStats { df: 1 }),\n1242\t            ]),\n1243\t        });\n1244\t\n1245\t        // Execute preflight to get global IDF\n1246\t        let preflight_req = PreflightRequest {\n1247\t            index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n1248\t            terms: vec![\"machine\".into(), \"learning\".into()],\n1249\t            filter: None,\n1250\t        };\n1251\t\n1252\t        let global_idf = execute_preflight(&plan, &c, &preflight_req, &topo).await.unwrap();\n1253\t\n1254\t        // Verify global aggregation\n1255\t        assert_eq!(global_idf.total_docs, 101_010);\n1256\t        assert_eq!(global_idf.terms.get(\"machine\").unwrap().df, 10_101);\n1257\t        assert_eq!(global_idf.terms.get(\"learning\").unwrap().df, 8_081);\n1258\t\n1259\t        // Verify global IDF is the same for all shards\n1260\t        // Expected IDF for \"machine\": log((101010 - 10101 + 0.5) / (10101 + 0.5)) + 1\n1261\t        let expected_idf_machine: f64 = ((101010.0_f64 - 10101.0 + 0.5) / (10101.0 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0;\n1262\t        let actual_idf_machine = global_idf.terms.get(\"machine\").unwrap().idf;\n1263\t        assert!((actual_idf_machine - expected_idf_machine).abs() < 0.001);\n1264\t\n1265\t        // Expected IDF for \"learning\": log((101010 - 8081 + 0.5) / (8081 + 0.5)) + 1\n1266\t        let expected_idf_learning: f64 = ((101010.0_f64 - 8081.0 + 0.5) / (8081.0 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0;\n1267\t        let actual_idf_learning = global_idf.terms.get(\"learning\").unwrap().idf;\n1268\t        assert!((actual_idf_learning - expected_idf_learning).abs() < 0.001);\n1269\t\n1270\t        // Verify that without global IDF, local IDF values would differ significantly\n1271\t        // Shard 0 local IDF for \"machine\": log((1000 - 100 + 0.5) / (100 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 3.19\n1272\t        // Shard 1 local IDF for \"machine\": log((100000 - 10000 + 0.5) / (10000 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 3.20\n1273\t        // Shard 2 local IDF for \"machine\": log((10 - 1 + 0.5) / (1 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.85\n1274\t        let local_idf_shard_0: f64 = ((1000.0_f64 - 100.0 + 0.5) / (100.0 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0;\n1275\t        let local_idf_shard_2: f64 = ((10.0_f64 - 1.0 + 0.5) / (1.0 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0;\n1276\t        assert!((local_idf_shard_2 - local_idf_shard_0).abs() > 0.2, \"Local IDF values should differ significantly\");\n1277\t        assert!((local_idf_shard_2 - actual_idf_machine).abs() > 0.3, \"Global IDF should be closer to large-shard local IDF\");\n1278\t    }\n1279\t\n1280\t    /// Test that DFS preflight handles empty query terms gracefully.\n1281\t    #[tokio::test]\n1282\t    async fn test_dfs_empty_query_terms() {\n1283\t        let topo = make_test_topology();\n1284\t        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 64, None).await;\n1285\t        let c = MockNodeClient::default();\n1286\t\n1287\t        let preflight_req = PreflightRequest {\n1288\t            index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n1289\t            terms: vec![],\n1290\t that to_node_body correctly injects showRankingScore: true and sets limit to offset + limit.\n1339\t    #[test]\n1340\t    fn test_to_node_body_injects_show_ranking_score() {\n1341\t        let req = SearchRequest {\n1342\t            index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n1343\t            query: Some(\"rust programming\".into()),\n1344\t            offset: 10,\n1345\t            limit: 20,\n1346\t #[tokio::test]\n1659\t    async fn test_plan_for_group_rotation() {\n1660\t        let mut topo = Topology::new(64, 1, 3);\n1661\t        for i in 0u32..4 {\n1662\t            topo.add_node(Node::new(\n1663\t                NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{i}\")),\n1664\t                format!(\"http://node-{i}:7700\"),\n1665\t                0,\n1666\t            ));\n1667\t .count();\n1676\t        let node_1_usages = plan.shard_to_node.values()\n1677\t            .filter(|n| n.as_str() == \"node-1\")\n1678\t            .count();\n1679\t        let node_2_usages = replicas[0]\n1684\t        // The assignment should distribute shards across nodes\n1685\t        // Total should be 64 across all nodes\n1686\t        let node_3_usages = plan.shard_to_node.values()\n1687\t            .filter(|n| n.as_str() == \"node-3\")\n1688\t            .count();\n1689\t        assert!(node_0_usages + node_1_usages + node_2_usages + node_3_usages == 64);\n1690\t    }\n1691\t\n1692\t    // ── plan_search_scatter_adaptive tests ───────────────────────────────────────\n1693\t\n1694\t    #[tokio::test]\n1695\t    async fn
