Document write path: add, replace, and delete documents.\n//!\n//! Implements P2.2 write path:\n//! - Primary key extraction on the hot path\n//! - `_miroir_shard` injection\n//! - Reserved field rejection\n//! - Two-rule quorum\n//!\n//! Implements P2.5 task reconciliation:\n//! - Collects per-node task UIDs\n//! - Registers Miroir task ID (mtask-<uuid>)\n//! - Returns mtask ID to client\n//!\n//! Implements §13.6 session pinning:\n//! - Records writes with session header\n//! - Tracks pinned group (first to reach quorum)\n\nuse axum::extract::{Extension, Path, Query};\nuse axum::response::{IntoResponse, Response};\nuse axum::http::{StatusCode, header};\nuse axum::{Json, Router};\nuse miroir_core::api_error::{MiroirCode, MeilisearchError};\nuse miroir_core::router::{shard_for_key, write_targets_with_migration};\nuse miroir_core::scatter::{DeleteByIdsRequest, DeleteByFilterRequest, NodeClient, WriteRequest, WriteResponse};\nuse miroir_core::task::TaskRegistry;\nuse miroir_core::topology::{Topology, NodeId};\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse serde_json::Value;\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse tracing::{debug, info, instrument};\n\nuse crate::client::HttpClient;\nuse crate::middleware::SessionId;\nuse crate::routes::admin_endpoints::AppState;\n\n/// Document write parameters from query string.\n#[derive(Debug, Deserialize)]\npub struct DocumentsParams {\n    primaryKey: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Task response (Meilisearch-compatible).\n#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]\npub struct TaskResponse {\n    taskUid: u64,\n    indexUid: String,\n    status: String,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    error: Option<Value>,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    #[serde(rename = \"type\")]\n    error_type: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Response for write operations.\n#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]\npub struct DocumentsWriteResponse {\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    taskUid: Option<String>, // Changed to String to hold mtask-<uuid>\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    indexUid: Option<String>,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    status: Option<String>,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    error: Option<String>,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    #[serde(rename = \"type\")]\n    error_type: Option<String>,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    code: Option<String>,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    link: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Header name for degraded write responses.\npub const HEADER_MIROIR_DEGRADED: &str = \"X-Miroir-Degraded\";\n\n/// Quorum tracking state for write operations.\n#[derive(Debug, Default)]\nstruct QuorumState {\n    /// Per-group ACK counts: group_id -> successful_ack_count\n    group_acks: HashMap<u32, usize>,\n    /// Per-group total node counts: group_id -> total_nodes_attempted\n    group_totals: HashMap<u32, usize>,\n    /// Groups that met quorum: group_id -> true\n    groups_met_quorum: HashMap<u32, bool>,\n    /// Total degraded groups count\n    degraded_groups: u32,\n}\n\nimpl QuorumState {\n    /// Record a write attempt to a node.\n    fn record_attempt(&mut self, group_id: u32, _node_id: &NodeId) {\n        *self.group_totals.entry(group_id).or_insert(0) += 1;\n    }\n\n    /// Record a successful write ACK from a node.\n    fn record_success(&mut self, group_id: u32, _node_id: &NodeId) {\n        *self.group_acks.entry(group_id).or_insert(0) += 1;\n    }\n\n    /// Record a failed write attempt from a node.\n    fn record_failure(&mut self, _group_id: u32) {\n        // Track that this group had a failure\n        // Degraded is determined after checking quorum\n    }\n\n    /// Check if a group has met quorum: floor(RF/2) + 1 ACKs required.\n    fn check_group_quorum(&mut self, group_id: u32, rf: usize) -> bool {\n        let acks = *self.group_acks.get(&group_id).unwrap_or(&0);\n        let quorum = (rf / 2) + 1;\n        let met = acks >= quorum;\n        *self.groups_met_quorum.entry(group_id).or_insert(false) = met;\n        met\n    }\n\n    /// Count how many groups met quorum.\n    fn count_quorum_groups(&self) -> usize {\n        self.groups_met_quorum.values().filter(|&&v| v).count()\n    }\n\n    /// Count degraded groups (groups that exist but didn't meet quorum).\n    fn count_degraded_groups(&mut self, replica_group_count: u32, rf: usize) -> u32 {\n        let mut degraded = 0u32;\n        for group_id in 0..replica_group_count {\n            if !self.check_group_quorum(group_id, rf) {\n                // Only count as degraded if we attempted to write to this group\n                if self.group_totals.contains_key(&group_id) {\n                    degraded += 1;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n        degraded\n    }\n}\n\n/// Build router for document endpoints.\npub fn router<S>() -> Router<S>\nwhere\n    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n{\n    Router::new()\n        .route(\"/\", axum::routing::post(post_documents))\n        .route(\"/\", axum::routing::put(put_documents))\n        .route(\"/\", axum::routing::delete(delete_documents))\n        .route(\"/:id\", axum::routing::delete(delete_document_by_id))\n}\n\n/// POST /indexes/{uid}/documents - Add documents.\n#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %index))]\nasync fn post_documents(\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n    Query(params): Query<DocumentsParams>,\n    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n    session_id: Option<Extension<crate::middleware::SessionId>>,\n    headers: axum::http::HeaderMap,\n    Json(documents): Json<Vec<Value>>,\n) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n    // Extract session ID from request extensions (set by session_pinning_middleware)\n    let sid = session_id.and_then(|ext| {\n        let s = ext.0;\n        if s.0.is_empty() { None } else { Some(s.0.clone()) }\n    });\n\n    // Extract idempotency key (plan §13.10)\n    let idempotency_key = headers\n        .get(\"Idempotency-Key\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n        .map(|s| s.to_string());\n\n    write_documents_impl(index, params.primaryKey, documents, &state, sid, idempotency_key).await\n}\n\n/// PUT /indexes/{uid}/documents - Replace documents.\n#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %index))]\nasync fn put_documents(\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n    Query(params): Query<DocumentsParams>,\n    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n    session_id: Option<Extension<crate::middleware::SessionId>>,\n    headers: axum::http::HeaderMap,\n    Json(documents): Json<Vec<Value>>,\n) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n    let sid = session_id.and_then(|ext| {\n        let s = ext.0;\n        if s.0.is_empty() { None } else { Some(s.0.clone()) }\n    });\n\n    // Extract idempotency key (plan §13.10)\n    let idempotency_key = headers\n        .get(\"Idempotency-Key\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n        .map(|s| s.to_string());\n\n    write_documents_impl(index, params.primaryKey, documents, &state, sid, idempotency_key).await\n}\n\n/// DELETE /indexes/{uid}/documents - Delete by IDs or filter.\nasync fn delete_documents(\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n    session_id: Option<Extension<crate::middleware::SessionId>>,\n    headers: axum::http::HeaderMap,\n    Json(body): Json<Value>,\n) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n    let sid = session_id.and_then(|ext| {\n        let s = ext.0;\n        if s.0.is_empty() { None } else { Some(s.0.clone()) }\n    });\n\n    // Extract idempotency key (plan §13.10)\n    let idempotency_key = headers\n        .get(\"Idempotency-Key\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n        .map(|s| s.to_string());\n\n    // Try to parse as delete by filter first\n    if let Some(filter) = body.get(\"filter\") {\n        let req = DeleteByFilterRequest {\n            index_uid: index.clone(),\n            filter: filter.clone(),\n            origin: None, // Client write\n        };\n        return delete_by_filter_impl(index, req, &state, sid, idempotency_key).await;\n    }\n\n    // Try to parse as delete by IDs\n    if let Some(ids) = body.get(\"ids\").and_then(|v| v.as_array()) {\n        let ids: Vec<String> = ids\n            .iter()\n            .filter_map(|v| v.as_str().map(|s| s.to_string()))\n            .collect();\n        if !ids.is_empty() {\n            let req = DeleteByIdsRequest {\n                index_uid: index.clone(),\n                ids,\n                origin: None, // Client write\n            };\n            return delete_by_ids_impl(index, req, &state, sid, idempotency_key).await;\n        }\n    }\n\n    // If we get here, the request body is malformed\n    Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n        MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n        \"delete request must include either 'filter' or 'ids' field\",\n    ))\n}\n\n/// DELETE /indexes/{uid}/documents/{id} - Delete single document by ID.\nasync fn delete_document_by_id(\n    Path((index, id)): Path<(String, String)>,\n    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n    session_id: Option<Extension<crate::middleware::SessionId>>,\n    headers: axum::http::HeaderMap,\n) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n    let sid = session_id.and_then(|ext| {\n        let s = ext.0;\n        if s.0.is_empty() { None } else { Some(s.0.clone()) }\n    });\n\n    // Extract idempotency key (plan §13.10)\n    let idempotency_key = headers\n        .get(\"Idempotency-Key\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n        .map(|s| s.to_string());\n\n    let req = DeleteByIdsRequest {\n        index_uid: index.clone(),\n        ids: vec![id],\n        origin: None, // Client write\n    };\n    delete_by_ids_impl(index, req, &state, sid, idempotency_key).await\n}\n\n/// Implementation for write documents (POST/PUT).\n#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %index, session_id))]\nasync fn write_documents_impl(\n    index: String,\n    primary_key: Option<String>,\n    mut documents: Vec<Value>,\n    state: &AppState,\n    session_id: Option<String>,\n    idempotency_key: Option<String>,\n) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n    if documents.is_empty() {\n        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n            \"cannot write empty document batch\",\n        ));\n    }\n\n    // 0.5. Check idempotency cache (plan §13.10)\n    if state.config.idempotency.enabled {\n        if let Some(ref key) = idempotency_key {\n            // Compute SHA256 hash of the request body\n            use sha2::{Digest, Sha256};\n            let body_hash = format!(\"{:x}\", Sha256::digest(serde_json::to_string(&documents).unwrap_or_default()));\n\n            // Check cache\n            match state.idempotency_cache.check(key, &body_hash).await {\n                Ok(Some(cached_mtask_id)) => {\n                    // Idempotency hit: return cached mtask ID\n                    state.metrics.inc_idempotency_hit(\"dedup\");\n                    return build_response_with_degraded_header(\n                        DocumentsWriteResponse {\n                            taskUid: Some(cached_mtask_id),\n                            indexUid: Some(index.clone()),\n                            status: Some(\"enqueued\".to_string()),\n                            error: None,\n                            error_type: None,\n                            code: None,\n                            link: None,\n                        },\n                        0, // No degraded groups for cached response\n                    );\n                }\n                Ok(None) => {\n                    // Cache miss - proceed with processing\n                    state.metrics.inc_idempotency_hit(\"miss\");\n                }\n                Err(miroir_core::error::MiroirError::IdempotencyKeyReused) => {\n                    // Key exists but body hash differs\n                    state.metrics.inc_idempotency_hit(\"conflict\");\n                    return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                        MiroirCode::IdempotencyKeyReused,\n                        \"idempotency key was already used with a different request body\",\n                    ));\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    // Other error - log but proceed (best-effort caching)\n                    tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"idempotency cache check failed, proceeding with write\");\n                    state.metrics.inc_idempotency_hit(\"miss\");\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // 1. Resolve alias to concrete index UID (plan §13.7)\n    // Aliases are resolved before any processing; writes to multi-target aliases are rejected\n    let index_uid = if state.config.aliases.enabled {\n        // Check if the index is an alias\n        let resolved = state.alias_registry.resolve(&index).await;\n        if resolved != vec![index.clone()] {\n            // It was an alias\n            // Check if it's a multi-target alias (read-only, ILM-managed)\n            if state.alias_registry.is_multi_target_alias(&index).await {\n                return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                    MiroirCode::MultiAliasNotWritable,\n                    format!(\n                        \"alias '{}' is a multi-target alias and is read-only (managed by ILM); writes must go to the concrete index or the write alias\",\n                        index\n                    ),\n                ));\n            }\n            // Single-target alias: record resolution metric and use the target\n            state.metrics.inc_alias_resolution(&index);\n            resolved.into_iter().next().unwrap_or_else(|| index.clone())\n        } else {\n            // Not an alias, use the index name as-is\n            index.clone()\n        }\n    } else {\n        index.clone()\n    };\n\n    // 1. Extract primary key from first document if not provided\n    let primary_key = primary_key.or_else(|| {\n        documents\n            .first()\n            .and_then(|doc| extract_primary_key(doc))\n    });\n\n    let primary_key = primary_key.ok_or_else(|| {\n        MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n            format!(\"primary key required for index `{}`\", index),\n        )\n    })?;\n\n    // 2. Validate all documents have the primary key and check for reserved field\n    let anti_entropy_enabled = state.config.anti_entropy.enabled;\n    let updated_at_field = &state.config.anti_entropy.updated_at_field;\n    let ttl_enabled = state.config.ttl.enabled;\n    let expires_at_field = &state.config.ttl.expires_at_field;\n\n    for (i, doc) in documents.iter().enumerate() {\n        // Check for reserved field BEFORE checking primary key (per acceptance criteria)\n        // _miroir_shard is ALWAYS reserved (plan §5)\n        if doc.get(\"_miroir_shard\").is_some() {\n            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n                \"document contains reserved field `_miroir_shard`\",\n            ));\n        }\n\n        // _miroir_updated_at is reserved ONLY when anti_entropy.enabled: true (plan §5, §13.8)\n        if anti_entropy_enabled && doc.get(updated_at_field).is_some() {\n            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n                format!(\"document contains reserved field `{}` (reserved when anti_entropy.enabled: true)\", updated_at_field),\n            ));\n        }\n\n        // _miroir_expires_at is reserved ONLY when ttl.enabled: true (plan §5, §13.14)\n        // When reserved, clients cannot SET it; the orchestrator controls it. When disabled,\n        // client values pass through end-to-end.\n        if ttl_enabled && doc.get(expires_at_field).is_some() {\n            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n                format!(\"document contains reserved field `{}` (reserved when ttl.enabled: true)\", expires_at_field),\n            ));\n        }\n\n        if doc.get(&primary_key).is_none() {\n            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n                format!(\n                    \"document at index {} missing primary key field `{}`\",\n                    i, primary_key\n                ),\n            ));\n        }\n    }\n\n    // 3. Inject _miroir_shard and _miroir_updated_at into each document\n    let topology = state.topology.read().await;\n    let shard_count = topology.shards;\n    let rf = topology.rf();\n    let replica_group_count = topology.replica_group_count();\n\n    // Get current timestamp in milliseconds since epoch for _miroir_updated_at stamping\n    let now_ms = if anti_entropy_enabled {\n        Some(\n            std::time::SystemTime::now()\n                .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n                .unwrap_or_default()\n                .as_millis() as u64\n        )\n    } else {\n        None\n    };\n\n    for doc in &mut documents {\n        if let Some(pk_value) = doc.get(&primary_key).and_then(|v| v.as_str()) {\n            let shard_id = shard_for_key(pk_value, shard_count);\n            doc[\"_miroir_shard\"] = serde_json::json!(shard_id);\n        }\n\n        // Stamp _miroir_updated_at when anti_entropy is enabled (plan §13.8)\n        // This happens AFTER reserved field validation, so orchestrator-controlled injection is allowed\n        if let Some(timestamp) = now_ms {\n            doc[updated_at_field] = serde_json::json!(timestamp);\n        }\n    }\n\n    // 4. Group documents by target nodes (per-batch grouping for efficient fan-out)\n    let node_documents = group_documents_by_shard(&documents, &primary_key, &topology)?;\n\n    // 5. Fan out to nodes and track quorum\n    let client = HttpClient::new(\n        state.config.node_master_key.clone(),\n        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n    );\n\n    let mut quorum_state = QuorumState::default();\n    let mut node_task_uids: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();\n\n    // For each shard, write to all RF nodes in each replica group (with dual-write support)\n    for (shard_id, docs) in node_documents {\n        // Get migration coordinator reference for dual-write detection\n        let migration_coordinator = state.migration_coordinator.as_ref().map(|c| {\n            // We need a read lock on the coordinator\n            tokio::task::block_in_place(|| {\n                tokio::runtime::Handle::current().block_on(async {\n                    c.read().await\n                })\n            })\n        });\n\n        // Use migration-aware routing\n        let targets = write_targets_with_migration(\n            shard_id,\n            &topology,\n            migration_coordinator.as_deref(),\n        );\n\n        if targets.is_empty() {\n            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n                format!(\"no available nodes for shard {}\", shard_id),\n            ));\n        }\n\n        // Track which groups we're targeting for this shard\n\n        for node_id in targets {\n            let node = topology\n                .node(&node_id)\n                .ok_or_else(|| MeilisearchError::new(MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable, \"node not found in topology\"))?;\n\n            let group_id = node.replica_group;\n            quorum_state.record_attempt(group_id, &node_id);\n\n            let req = WriteRequest {\n                index_uid: index_uid.clone(),\n                documents: docs.clone(),\n                primary_key: Some(primary_key.clone()),\n                origin: None, // Client write\n            };\n\n            match client.write_documents(&node_id, &node.address, &req).await {\n                Ok(resp) if resp.success => {\n                    quorum_state.record_success(group_id, &node_id);\n                    if let Some(task_uid) = resp.task_uid {\n                        node_task_uids.insert(node_id.as_str().to_string(), task_uid);\n                    }\n                }\n                Ok(resp) => {\n                    // Non-success response (validation error, etc.)\n                    return Ok(build_json_error_response(build_error_response(resp)));\n                }\n                Err(_) => {\n                    quorum_state.record_failure(group_id);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // 6. Apply two-rule quorum logic\n    let degraded_groups = quorum_state.count_degraded_groups(replica_group_count, rf);\n    let quorum_groups = quorum_state.count_quorum_groups();\n\n    // Write success if at least one group met quorum\n    if quorum_groups == 0 {\n        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n            \"no replica group met quorum\",\n        ));\n    }\n\n    // 6.5. Find the first group to reach quorum (for session pinning, plan §13.6)\n    // Groups are checked in ascending order, so the first one with quorum is the first\n    let first_quorum_group = (0..replica_group_count)\n        .find(|&group_id| {\n            let acks = *quorum_state.group_acks.get(&group_id).unwrap_or(&0);\n            let quorum = (rf / 2) + 1;\n            acks >= quorum\n        })\n        .unwrap_or(0); // Default to group 0 if somehow no quorum (shouldn't happen here)\n\n    // 7. Register Miroir task with collected node task UIDs\n    let miroir_task = state\n        .task_registry\n        .register_with_metadata(\n            node_task_uids.clone(),\n            Some(index_uid.clone()),\n            Some(\"documentAdditionOrUpdate\".to_string()),\n        )\n        .map_err(|e| MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n            format!(\"failed to register task: {}\", e),\n        ))?;\n\n    // 7.5. Record session pinning if session header present (plan §13.6)\n    if let (Some(ref sid), true) = (&session_id, state.session_manager.is_enabled()) {\n        if let Err(e) = state.session_manager.record_write_with_quorum(\n            sid,\n            miroir_task.miroir_id.clone(),\n            first_quorum_group,\n        ).await {\n            // Log error but don't fail the write - session pinning is best-effort\n            tracing::error!(\n                session_id = %sid,\n                error = %e,\n                \"failed to record session pinning for write\"\n            );\n        }\n    }\n\n    // 7.6. Insert into idempotency cache if key was provided (plan §13.10)\n    if state.config.idempotency.enabled {\n        if let Some(ref key) = idempotency_key {\n            use sha2::{Digest, Sha256};\n            let body_hash = format!(\"{:x}\", Sha256::digest(serde_json::to_string(&documents).unwrap_or_default()));\n            state.idempotency_cache.insert(\n                key.clone(),\n                body_hash,\n                miroir_task.miroir_id.clone(),\n            ).await;\n            // Update cache size metric\n            state.metrics.set_idempotency_cache_size(state.idempotency_cache.size().await as u64);\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Build success response with degraded header and mtask ID\n    build_response_with_degraded_header(\n        DocumentsWriteResponse {\n            taskUid: Some(miroir_task.miroir_id),\n            indexUid: Some(index_uid.clone()),\n            status: Some(\"enqueued\".to_string()),\n            error: None,\n            error_type: None,\n            code: None,\n            link: None,\n        },\n        degraded_groups,\n    )\n}\n\n/// Implementation for delete by IDs.\nasync fn delete_by_ids_impl(\n    index: String,\n    req: DeleteByIdsRequest,\n    state: &AppState,\n    session_id: Option<String>,\n    idempotency_key: Option<String>,\n) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n    if req.ids.is_empty() {\n        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n            \"cannot delete empty ID list\",\n        ));\n    }\n\n    // 0.5. Check idempotency cache (plan §13.10)\n    if state.config.idempotency.enabled {\n        if let Some(ref key) = idempotency_key {\n            use sha2::{Digest, Sha256};\n            let body_hash = format!(\"{:x}\", Sha256::digest(serde_json::to_string(&req).unwrap_or_default()));\n\n            match state.idempotency_cache.check(key, &body_hash).await {\n                Ok(Some(cached_mtask_id)) => {\n                    state.metrics.inc_idempotency_hit(\"dedup\");\n                    return build_response_with_degraded_header(\n                        DocumentsWriteResponse {\n                            taskUid: Some(cached_mtask_id),\n                            indexUid: Some(index.clone()),\n                            status: Some(\"enqueued\".to_string()),\n                            error: None,\n                            error_type: None,\n                            code: None,\n                            link: None,\n                        },\n                        0,\n                    );\n                }\n                Ok(None) => {\n                    state.metrics.inc_idempotency_hit(\"miss\");\n                }\n                Err(miroir_core::error::MiroirError::IdempotencyKeyReused) => {\n                    state.metrics.inc_idempotency_hit(\"conflict\");\n                    return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                        MiroirCode::IdempotencyKeyReused,\n                        \"idempotency key was already used with a different request body\",\n                    ));\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"idempotency cache check failed, proceeding with delete\");\n                    state.metrics.inc_idempotency_hit(\"miss\");\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Resolve alias to concrete index UID (plan §13.7)\n    let index_uid = if state.config.aliases.enabled {\n        let resolved = state.alias_registry.resolve(&index).await;\n        if resolved != vec![index.clone()] {\n            // It was an alias - check if it's a multi-target alias (read-only)\n            if state.alias_registry.is_multi_target_alias(&index).await {\n                return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                    MiroirCode::MultiAliasNotWritable,\n                    format!(\n                        \"alias '{}' is a multi-target alias and is read-only (managed by ILM); deletes must go to the concrete index or the write alias\",\n                        index\n                    ),\n                ));\n            }\n            // Single-target alias: record resolution metric and use the target\n            state.metrics.inc_alias_resolution(&index);\n            resolved.into_iter().next().unwrap_or_else(|| index.clone())\n        } else {\n            index.clone()\n        }\n    } else {\n        index.clone()\n    };\n\n    let topology = state.topology.read().await;\n    let rf = topology.rf();\n    let replica_group_count = topology.replica_group_count();\n\n    // Group IDs by target shard for independent per-shard routing\n    let mut shard_ids: HashMap<u32, Vec<String>> = HashMap::new();\n    for id in &req.ids {\n        let shard_id = shard_for_key(id, topology.shards);\n        shard_ids.entry(shard_id).or_default().push(id.clone());\n    }\n\n    let client = HttpClient::new(\n        state.config.node_master_key.clone(),\n        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n    );\n\n    let mut quorum_state = QuorumState::default();\n    let mut node_task_uids: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();\n\n    // For each shard, write to all RF nodes in each replica group\n    for (shard_id, ids) in shard_ids {\n        let targets = miroir_core::router::write_targets(shard_id, &topology);\n\n        if targets.is_empty() {\n            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n                format!(\"no available nodes for shard {}\", shard_id),\n            ));\n        }\n\n        for node_id in targets {\n            let node = topology\n                .node(&node_id)\n                .ok_or_else(|| MeilisearchError::new(MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable, \"node not found in topology\"))?;\n\n            let group_id = node.replica_group;\n            quorum_state.record_attempt(group_id, &node_id);\n\n            let delete_req = DeleteByIdsRequest {\n                index_uid: index_uid.clone(),\n                ids: ids.clone(),\n                origin: None, // Client write\n            };\n\n            match client.delete_documents(&node_id, &node.address, &delete_req).await {\n                Ok(resp) if resp.success => {\n                    quorum_state.record_success(group_id, &node_id);\n                    if let Some(task_uid) = resp.task_uid {\n                        node_task_uids.insert(node_id.as_str().to_string(), task_uid);\n                    }\n                }\n                Ok(resp) => {\n                    return Ok(build_json_error_response(build_error_response(resp)));\n                }\n                Err(_) => {\n                    quorum_state.record_failure(group_id);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Apply two-rule quorum logic\n    let degraded_groups = quorum_state.count_degraded_groups(replica_group_count, rf);\n    let quorum_groups = quorum_state.count_quorum_groups();\n\n    // Write success if at least one group met quorum\n    if quorum_groups == 0 {\n        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n            \"no replica group met quorum\",\n        ));\n    }\n\n    // Find the first group to reach quorum (for session pinning, plan §13.6)\n    let first_quorum_group = (0..replica_group_count)\n        .find(|&group_id| {\n            let acks = *quorum_state.group_acks.get(&group_id).unwrap_or(&0);\n            let quorum = (rf / 2) + 1;\n            acks >= quorum\n        })\n        .unwrap_or(0);\n\n    // Register Miroir task with collected node task UIDs\n    let miroir_task = state\n        .task_registry\n        .register_with_metadata(\n            node_task_uids.clone(),\n            Some(index.clone()),\n            Some(\"documentDeletion\".to_string()),\n        )\n        .map_err(|e| MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n            format!(\"failed to register task: {}\", e),\n        ))?;\n\n    // Record session pinning if session header present (plan §13.6)\n    if let (Some(ref sid), true) = (&session_id, state.session_manager.is_enabled()) {\n        if let Err(e) = state.session_manager.record_write_with_quorum(\n            sid,\n            miroir_task.miroir_id.clone(),\n            first_quorum_group,\n        ).await {\n            tracing::error!(\n                session_id = %sid,\n                error = %e,\n                \"failed to record session pinning for delete\"\n            );\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Insert into idempotency cache if key was provided (plan §13.10)\n    if state.config.idempotency.enabled {\n        if let Some(ref key) = idempotency_key {\n            use sha2::{Digest, Sha256};\n            let body_hash = format!(\"{:x}\", Sha256::digest(serde_json::to_string(&req).unwrap_or_default()));\n            state.idempotency_cache.insert(\n                key.clone(),\n                body_hash,\n                miroir_task.miroir_id.clone(),\n            ).await;\n            state.metrics.set_idempotency_cache_size(state.idempotency_cache.size().await as u64);\n        }\n    }\n\n    build_response_with_degraded_header(\n        DocumentsWriteResponse {\n            taskUid: Some(miroir_task.miroir_id),\n            indexUid: Some(index.clone()),\n            status: Some(\"enqueued\".to_string()),\n            error: None,\n            error_type: None,\n            code: None,\n            link: None,\n        },\n        degraded_groups,\n    )\n}\n\n/// Implementation for delete by filter (broadcast to all nodes).\nasync fn delete_by_filter_impl(\n    index: String,\n    req: DeleteByFilterRequest,\n    state: &AppState,\n    session_id: Option<String>,\n    idempotency_key: Option<String>,\n) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n    // 0.5. Check idempotency cache (plan §13.10)\n    if state.config.idempotency.enabled {\n        if let Some(ref key) = idempotency_key {\n            use sha2::{Digest, Sha256};\n            let body_hash = format!(\"{:x}\", Sha256::digest(serde_json::to_string(&req).unwrap_or_default()));\n\n            match state.idempotency_cache.check(key, &body_hash).await {\n                Ok(Some(cached_mtask_id)) => {\n                    state.metrics.inc_idempotency_hit(\"dedup\");\n                    return build_response_with_degraded_header(\n                        DocumentsWriteResponse {\n                            taskUid: Some(cached_mtask_id),\n                            indexUid: Some(index.clone()),\n                            status: Some(\"enqueued\".to_string()),\n                            error: None,\n                            error_type: None,\n                            code: None,\n                            link: None,\n                        },\n                        0,\n                    );\n                }\n                Ok(None) => {\n                    state.metrics.inc_idempotency_hit(\"miss\");\n                }\n                Err(miroir_core::error::MiroirError::IdempotencyKeyReused) => {\n                    state.metrics.inc_idempotency_hit(\"conflict\");\n                    return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                        MiroirCode::IdempotencyKeyReused,\n                        \"idempotency key was already used with a different request body\",\n                    ));\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"idempotency cache check failed, proceeding with delete by filter\");\n                    state.metrics.inc_idempotency_hit(\"miss\");\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    let topology = state.topology.read().await;\n    let rf = topology.rf();\n    let replica_group_count = topology.replica_group_count();\n\n    let client = HttpClient::new(\n        state.config.node_master_key.clone(),\n        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n    );\n\n    let mut quorum_state = QuorumState::default();\n    let mut node_task_uids: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();\n\n    // Broadcast to all nodes (cannot shard-route for filters)\n    for node in topology.nodes() {\n        let group_id = node.replica_group;\n        quorum_state.record_attempt(group_id, &node.id);\n\n        match client\n            .delete_documents_by_filter(&node.id, &node.address, &req)\n            .await\n        {\n            Ok(resp) if resp.success => {\n                quorum_state.record_success(group_id, &node.id);\n                if let Some(task_uid) = resp.task_uid {\n                    node_task_uids.insert(node.id.as_str().to_string(), task_uid);\n                }\n            }\n            Ok(resp) => {\n                return Ok(build_json_error_response(build_error_response(resp)));\n            }\n            Err(_) => {\n                quorum_state.record_failure(group_id);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Apply two-rule quorum logic\n    let degraded_groups = quorum_state.count_degraded_groups(replica_group_count, rf);\n    let quorum_groups = quorum_state.count_quorum_groups();\n\n    // Write success if at least one group met quorum\n    if quorum_groups == 0 {\n        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n            \"no replica group met quorum\",\n        ));\n    }\n\n    // Find the first group to reach quorum (for session pinning, plan §13.6)\n    let first_quorum_group = (0..replica_group_count)\n        .find(|&group_id| {\n            let acks = *quorum_state.group_acks.get(&group_id).unwrap_or(&0);\n            let quorum = (rf / 2) + 1;\n            acks >= quorum\n        })\n        .unwrap_or(0);\n\n    // Register Miroir task with collected node task UIDs\n    let miroir_task = state\n        .task_registry\n        .register_with_metadata(\n            node_task_uids.clone(),\n            Some(index.clone()),\n            Some(\"documentDeletion\".to_string()),\n        )\n        .map_err(|e| MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n            format!(\"failed to register task: {}\", e),\n        ))?;\n\n    // Record session pinning if session header present (plan §13.6)\n    if let (Some(ref sid), true) = (&session_id, state.session_manager.is_enabled()) {\n        if let Err(e) = state.session_manager.record_write_with_quorum(\n            sid,\n            miroir_task.miroir_id.clone(),\n            first_quorum_group,\n        ).await {\n            tracing::error!(\n                session_id = %sid,\n                error = %e,\n                \"failed to record session pinning for delete by filter\"\n            );\n        }\n    }\n\n    build_response_with_degraded_header(\n        DocumentsWriteResponse {\n            taskUid: Some(miroir_task.miroir_id),\n            indexUid: Some(index.clone()),\n            status: Some(\"enqueued\".to_string()),\n            error: None,\n            error_type: None,\n            code: None,\n            link: None,\n        },\n        degraded_groups,\n    )\n}\n\n/// Extract primary key from a document by checking common field names.\n///\n/// Tries fields in order: id, pk, key, _id.\nfn extract_primary_key(doc: &Value) -> Option<String> {\n    [\"id\", \"pk\", \"key\", \"_id\"]\n        .iter()\n        .find(|&&key| doc.get(key).is_some())\n        .map(|&s| s.to_string())\n}\n\n/// Group documents by their target shard for fan-out optimization.\n///\n/// Returns a map of shard_id -> documents to send to that shard.\n/// The caller then fans out each shard's documents to all RF nodes in each group.\n///\n/// This per-batch grouping minimizes HTTP fan-out count (critical at scale).\nfn group_documents_by_shard(\n    documents: &[Value],\n    primary_key: &str,\n    topology: &Topology,\n) -> std::result::Result<HashMap<u32, Vec<Value>>, MeilisearchError> {\n    let mut shard_documents: HashMap<u32, Vec<Value>> = HashMap::new();\n\n    for doc in documents {\n        let pk_value = doc\n            .get(primary_key)\n            .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n            .ok_or_else(|| {\n                MeilisearchError::new(\n                    MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n                    \"primary key value must be a string\",\n                )\n            })?;\n\n        let shard_id = shard_for_key(pk_value, topology.shards);\n        shard_documents\n            .entry(shard_id)\n            .or_default()\n            .push(doc.clone());\n    }\n\n    Ok(shard_documents)\n}\n\n/// Build an error response from a node error.\nfn build_error_response(resp: WriteResponse) -> DocumentsWriteResponse {\n    DocumentsWriteResponse {\n        taskUid: resp.task_uid.map(|uid| uid.to_string()),\n        indexUid: None,\n        status: None,\n        error: resp.message,\n        error_type: resp.error_type,\n        code: resp.code,\n        link: None,\n    }\n}\n\n/// Build a success response with optional X-Miroir-Degraded header.\nfn build_response_with_degraded_header(\n    response: DocumentsWriteResponse,\n    degraded_groups: u32,\n) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n    let body = serde_json::to_string(&response).map_err(|e| {\n        MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n            format!(\"failed to serialize response: {}\", e),\n        )\n    })?;\n\n    let mut builder = Response::builder()\n        .status(StatusCode::OK)\n        .header(header::CONTENT_TYPE, \"application/json\");\n\n    // Add X-Miroir-Degraded header if any groups were degraded\n    if degraded_groups > 0 {\n        builder = builder.header(HEADER_MIROIR_DEGRADED, format!(\"groups={}\", degraded_groups));\n    }\n\n    Ok(builder\n        .body(axum::body::Body::from(body))\n        .map_err(|e| MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n            format!(\"failed to build response: {}\", e),\n        ))?)\n}\n\n/// Build an error response as JSON (for forwarded node errors).\nfn build_json_error_response(resp: DocumentsWriteResponse) -> Response {\n    (\n        StatusCode::OK,\n        [(header::CONTENT_TYPE, \"application/json\")],\n        Json(resp),\n    )\n        .into_response()\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use miroir_core::api_error::{MiroirCode, MeilisearchError};\n    use serde_json::json;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_extract_primary_key_common_fields() {\n        let doc_with_id = serde_json::json!({\"id\": \"test123\", \"name\": \"Test\"});\n        assert_eq!(extract_primary_key(&doc_with_id), Some(\"id\".to_string()));\n\n        let doc_with_pk = serde_json::json!({\"pk\": \"test456\", \"name\": \"Test\"});\n        assert_eq!(extract_primary_key(&doc_with_pk), Some(\"pk\".to_string()));\n\n        let doc_with_key = serde_json::json!({\"key\": \"test789\", \"name\": \"Test\"});\n        assert_eq!(extract_primary_key(&doc_with_key), Some(\"key\".to_string()));\n\n        let doc_with__id = serde_json::json!({\"_id\": \"test000\", \"name\": \"Test\"});\n        assert_eq!(extract_primary_key(&doc_with__id), Some(\"_id\".to_string()));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_extract_primary_key_no_common_field() {\n        let doc = serde_json::json!({\"name\": \"Test\", \"value\": 42});\n        assert_eq!(extract_primary_key(&doc), None);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_extract_primary_key_priority() {\n        // Should return \"id\" first even if other fields exist\n        let doc = &str) -> MeilisearchError {\n        MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n            format!(\"document contains reserved field `{}`\", field),\n        )\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_reserved_field_miroir_shard_always_rejected() {\n        // _miroir_shard is ALWAYS reserved regardless of config\n        let err = reserved_field_error(\"_miroir_shard\");\n #[test]\n    fn test_reserved_field_miroir_updated_at_when_anti_entropy_enabled() {\n        // When anti_entropy.enabled: true, _miroir_updated_at is reserved\n        let field = tc.has_expires_at, \"{}: expires_at check\", tc.description);\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_reserved_field_error_format_matches_meilisearch_shape() {\n        let err = json!({\"_miroir_shard\": 1, \"name\": \"No PK\"});\n        assert!(doc_with_shard_no_pk.get(\"_miroir_shard\").is_some());\n        assert!(doc_with_shard_no_pk.get(\"id\").is_none());\n\n        // }\n\n    // P2.9: Complete reserved field matrix tests\n    //\n    // Matrix cells per plan §5:\n    // | Field           | Config disabled | Config enabled |\n    // {\n90\t        // Check cache first\n91\t        {\n92\t            let cache = self.version_cache.read().await;\n93\t            let last_update = self.last_cache_update.read().await;\n94\t            if let (Some(ref cached), Some(last)) = (cache.as_ref(), last_update.as_ref()) {\n95\t                if last.elapsed().as_secs() < self.cache_ttl_secs {\n96\t                    return Ok((**cached).clone());\n97\t                }\n98\t            }\n99\t        }\n100\t\n101\t        // Cache miss or expired - fetch from a node\n102\t        let client = Client::builder()\n103\t            .timeout(Duration::from_secs(2))\n104\t            .build()\n105\t            .map_err(|_| StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)?;\n106\t\n107\t        for address in &self.node_addresses {\n108\t            let url = format!(\"{}/version\", address.trim_end_matches('/'));\n109\t            let response = client\n110\t                .get(&url)\n111\t                .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", self.node_master_key))\n112\t                .send()\n113\t                .await;\n114\t\n115\t            if let Ok(resp) = response {\n116\t                if resp.status().is_success() {\n117\t                    if let Ok(body) = resp.text().await {\n118\t                        // Update cache\n119\t                        *self.version_cache.write().await = Some(body.clone());\n120\t                        *self.last_cache_update.write().await = Some(std::time::Instant::now());\n121\t                        return ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n132\t// Local Rate Limiter (for single-pod deployments)\n133\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n134\t\n135\t/// In-memory rate limiter for admin login (local backend only).\n136\t/// Thread-safe using Arc<Mutex<...>>.\n137\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n138\tpub struct LocalAdminRateLimiter {\n139\t    inner: Arc<std::sync::Mutex<LocalAdminRateLimiterInner>>,\n140\t}\n141\t\n142\t#[derive(Debug, Default)]\n143\tstruct LocalAdminRateLimiterInner {\n144\t    /// Map of IP -> (request_timestamps_ms, failed_count, backoff_until_ms)\n145\t    state: HashMap<String, LocalRateLimitState>,\n146\t}\n147\t\n148\t#[derive(Debug, Default, Clone)]\n149\tstruct LocalRateLimitState {\n150\t    /// Timestamps of recent requests (for sliding window)\n151\t    request_timestamps_ms: Vec<i64>,\n152\t    /// Consecutive failed login attempts\n153\t    failed_count: u32,\n154\t    /// Unix timestamp (ms) when backoff expires\n155\t    backoff_until_ms: Option<i64>,\n156\t}\n157\t\n158\timpl LocalAdminRateLimiter {\n159\t    pub fn new() -> Self {\n160\t        Self {\n161\t            inner: Arc::new(std::sync::Mutex::new(LocalAdminRateLimiterInner::default())),\n162\t        }\n163\t    }\n164\t\n165\t    /// Check rate limit and exponential backoff.\n166\t    /// Returns (allowed, wait_seconds).\n167\t    pub fn check(\n168\t        &self,\n169\t        ip: &str,\n170\t        limit: u64,\n171\t        window_ms: u64,\n172\t        failed_threshold: u32,\n173\t        backoff_start_minutes: u64,\n174\t        backoff_max_hours: u64,\n175\t    ) -> (bool, Option<u64>) {\n176\t        let mut inner = self.inner.lock().unwrap();\n177\t        let now = now_ms();\n178\t        let state = inner.state.entry(ip.to_string()).or_default();\n179\t\n180\t        // Check if we're in backoff mode\n181\t        if let Some(backoff_until) = state.backoff_until_ms {\n182\t            if backoff_until > now {\n183\t                let wait_seconds = ((backoff_until - now) / 1000) as u64;\n184\t                return (false, Some(wait_seconds));\n185\t            }\n186\t            // Backoff expired, clear it\n187\t            state.backoff_until_ms = None;\n188\t        }\n189\t\n190\t        // Clean old timestamps outside the window\n191\t        state.request_timestamps_ms.retain(|&ts| now - ts < window_ms as i64);\n192\t\n193\t        // Check if limit exceeded\n194\t        if state.request_timestamps_ms.len() >= limit as usize {\n195\t            // Enter backoff mode after threshold consecutive failures\n196\t            let failed = state.failed_count + 1;\n197\t            state.failed_count = failed;\n198\t\n199\t            if failed >= failed_threshold {\n200\t                let backoff_minutes = backoff_start_minutes * (1u64 << ((failed - failed_threshold) as u64).min(7)); // Cap at 2^7 = 128x\n201\t                let backoff_seconds = (backoff_minutes * 60).min(backoff_max_hours * 3600);\n202\t                state.backoff_until_ms = Some(now + (backoff_seconds as i64 * 1000));\n203\t                return (false, Some(backoff_seconds));\n204\t            }\n205\t\n206\t            return (false, None);\n207\t        }\n208\t\n209\t        // Record this request\n210\t        state.request_timestamps_ms.push(now);\n211\t        (true, None)\n212\t    }\n213\t\n214\t    /// Reset rate limit and backoff state on successful login.\n215\t    pub fn reset(&self, ip: &str) {\n216\t        let mut inner = self.inner.lock().unwrap();\n217\t        inner.state.remove(ip);\n218\t    }\n219\t\n220\t    /// Record a failed login attempt (for backoff calculation).\n221\t    pub fn record_failure(&self, ip: &str, failed_threshold: u32, backoff_start_minutes: u64, backoff_max_hours: u64) -> Option<u64> {\n222\t        let mut inner = self.inner.lock().unwrap();\n223\t        let now = now_ms();\n224\t        let state = inner.state.entry(ip.to_string()).or_default();\n225\t\n226\t        state.failed_count += 1;\n227\t\n228\t        if state.failed_count >= failed_threshold {\n229\t            let backoff_minutes = backoff_start_minutes * (1u64 << ((state.failed_count - failed_threshold) as u64).min(7));\n230\t            let backoff_seconds = (backoff_minutes * 60).min(backoff_max_hours * 3600);\n231\t            state.backoff_until_ms = Some(now + (backoff_seconds as i64 * 1000));\n232\t            return Some(backoff_seconds);\n233\t        }\n234\t\n235\t        None\n236\t    }\n237\t}\n238\t\n239\timpl Default for LocalAdminRateLimiter {\n240\t    fn default() -> Self {\n241\t        Self::new()\n242\t    }\n243\t}\n244\t\n245\t/// In-memory rate limiter for search UI (local backend only).\n246\t/// Thread-safe using Arc<Mutex<...>>.\n247\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n248\tpub struct {\n282\t            return (false, None);\n283\t        }\n284\t\n285\t        // Record this request\n286\t        timestamps.push(now);\n287\t        (true, None)\n288\t }\n683\t\n684\t    /// Check if a covering quorum is reachable.\n685\t    pub async fn check_covering_quorum(&self) -> bool {\n686\t        let topo = self.topology.read().await;\n687\t        let node_map = topo.node_map();\n688\t\n689\t        // For each replica group, check if we have enough healthy nodes\n690\t        for group in topo.groups() {\n691\t            let healthy = group.healthy_nodes(&node_map);\n692\t            let required = (topo.rf() + 1) / 2; // Simple majority for quorum\n693\t            if healthy.len() < required {\n694\t                return false;\n695\t            }\n696\t        }\n697\t\n698\t        true\n699\t    }\n700\t\n701\t    /// Sync rebalancer metrics to Prometheus (called from health checker).\n702\t    pub async fn   let in_progress = rebalancer_metrics.rebalance_start_time.is_some();\n706\t            self.metrics.set_rebalance_in_progress(in_progress);\n707\t\n708\t            // Calculate delta for documents migrated counter\n709\t            let current_total = rebalancer_metrics.documents_migrated_total;\n710\t            let previous = self.previous_docs_migrated.load(std::sync::atomic::Ordering::Relaxed);\n711\t            if current_total > previous {\n712\t                let delta = current_total - previous;\n713\t                self.metrics.inc_rebalance_documents_migrated(delta);\n714\t                self.previous_docs_migrated.store(current_total, std::sync::atomic::Ordering::Relaxed);\n715\t            }\n716\t\n717\t            let duration = rebalancer_metrics.current_duration_secs();\n718\t            if duration > 0.0 {\n719\t                self.metrics.observe_rebalance_duration(duration);\n720\t            }\n721\t        }\n722\t    }\n723\t}\n724\t\n725\t/// Response for GET /_miroir/topology (plan §10 JSON shape).\n726\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n727\tpub struct TopologyResponse fn get_topology<S>(State(state): State<S>) -> Result<Json<TopologyResponse>, StatusCode>\n756\twhere\n757\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n758\t    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n759\t{\n760\t    let rebalancer.status().await;\n769\t        status.in_progress\n770\t    } else {\n771\t        false\n772\t    };\n773\t\n774\t    // Build node info list\n775\t    let nodes: Vec<NodeInfo> = n.status).to_lowercase(),\n781\t            shard_count: 0, // TODO: compute from routing table\n782\t            last_seen_ms: 0, // TODO: track last health check time\n783\t            error: None,     // TODO: populate from last health check error\n784\t        })\n785\t        .collect();\n786\t\n787\t    // Check if fully covered\n788\t    let fully_covered = degraded_count == 0;\n789\t\n790\t    let response = TopologyResponse {\n791\t        shards: topo.shards,\n792\t        replication_factor: topo.rf() as u32,\n793\t        nodes,\n794\t        degraded_node_count: table.\n803\tpub async fn get_shards<S>(State(state): State<S>) -> Result<Json<ShardsResponse>, StatusCode>\n804\twhere\n805\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n806\t    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n807\t{\n808\t    let state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n809\t    let topo = state.topology.read().await;\n810\t    let mut shards = HashMap::new();\n811\t\n812\t    // Build shard -> node mapping using rendezvous hash\n813\t    for shard_id in 0..topo.shards {\n814\t        let mut node_ids = Vec::new();\n815\t\n816\t        // Collect nodes from all replica groups for this shard\n817\t        for group in topo.groups() {\n818\t            let assigned = router::assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), topo.rf());\n819\t            for node_id in assigned {\n820\t                node_ids.push(node_id.as_str().to_string());\n821\t            }\n822\t        }\n823\t\n824\t        shards.insert(shard_id.to_string(), node_ids);\n825\t    }\n826\t\n827\t    Ok(Json(ShardsResponse { shards }))\n828\t}\n829\t\n830\t/// GET /_miroir/ready — readiness probe (503 during startup, 200 once ready).\n831\tpub async fn get_ready<S>(State(state): State<S>) -> Result<&'static str, StatusCode>\n832\twhere\n833\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n834\t    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n835\t{\n836\t    let state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n837\t    let ready = *state.ready.read().await;\n838\t\n839\t    if ready {\n840\t        Ok(\"\")\n841\t    } else {\n842\t        // Not yet marked ready - check if covering quorum exists\n843\t        let has_quorum = %e, \"failed to encode metrics\");\n865\t            StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR.into_response()\n866\t        }\n867\t    }\n868\t}\n869\t\n870\t/// POST /_miroir/ui/search/{index}/rotate-scoped-key — manual rotation trigger.\n871\t///\n872\t/// Admin-gated endpoint that initiates a scoped key rotation for the given index.\n873\t/// Set `force: true` in the request body to bypass the timing gate.\n874\tpub async fn rotate_scoped_key_handler<S>(\n875\t    State(state): State<S>,\n876\t    Path(index): Path<String>,\n877\t    Json(body): Json<RotateScopedKeyRequest>,\n878\t) -> Result<Json<RotateScopedKeyResponse>, (StatusCode, String)>\n879\twhere\n880\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n881\t    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n882\t{\n883\t    let app_state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n884\t\n885\t    let redis = app_state.redis_store.clone().ok_or_else(|| {\n886\t        (\n887\t            StatusCode::PRECONDITION_FAILED,\n888\t            \"scoped key rotation requires Redis task store\".into(),\n889\t        )\n890\t    })?;\n891\t\n892\t    if !app_state.config.search_ui.enabled {\n893\t        return Err((\n894\t            StatusCode::PRECONDITION_FAILED,\n895\t            \"search_ui is not enabled\".into(),\n896\t        ));\n897\t    }\n898\t\n899\t    let rotation_state = ScopedKeyRotationState {\n900\t        config: app_state.config.clone(),\n901\t        redis,\n902\t        pod_id: app_state.pod_id.clone(),\n903\t    };\n904\t\n905\t    info!(\n906\t        index = %index,\n907\t        force = body.force,\n908\t        pod_id = %app_state.pod_id,\n909\t        \"manual scoped key rotation triggered\"\n910\t    );\n911\t\n912\t    match scoped_key_rotation::check_and_rotate(&rotation_state, &index, body.force).await {\n913\t        Ok(response) => Ok(Json(response)),\n914\t        Err(e) => {\n915\t            error!(index = %index, error = %e, \"manual scoped key rotation failed\");\n916\t            Err((StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, e))\n917\t        }\n918\t    }\n919\t}\n920\t\n921\t/// Parse a rate limit string like \"10/minute\" into (limit, window_seconds).\n922\tpub fn parse_rate_limit(s: &str) -> Result<(u64, u64), String> {\n923\t    let parts: Vec<&str> = s.split('/').collect();\n924\t    if parts.len() != 2 {\n925\t        return Err(format!(\"invalid rate limit format: '{}', expected 'N/UNIT'\", s));\n926\t    }\n927\t    let limit: u64 = parts[0].parse()\n928\t        .map_err(|_| format!(\"invalid limit number: '{}'\", parts[0]))?;\n929\t    let window_seconds = match parts[1] {\n930\t        \"second\" | \"s\" => 1,\n931\t        \"minute\" | \"m\" => 60,\n932\t        \"hour\" | \"h\" => 3600,\n933\t        \"day\" | \"d\" => 86400,\n934\t        unit => return Err(format!(\"invalid time unit: '{}', expected second/minute/hour/day\", unit)),\n935\t    };\n936\t    Ok((limit, window_seconds))\n937\t}\n938\t\n939\t/// Generate a random session ID.\n940\tfn generate_session_id() -> String {\n941\t    let mut bytes = [0u8; 24];\n942\t    rand::rngs::OsRng.fill_bytes(&mut bytes);\n943\t    hex::encode(&bytes)\n944\t}\n945\t\n946\t/// POST /_miroir/admin/login — admin login with rate limiting and exponential backoff.\n947\t///\n948\t/// Request body:\n949\t/// ```json\n950\t/// { \"admin_key\": \"...\" }\n951\t/// ```\n952\t///\n953\t/// On success, sets a `miroir_admin_session` cookie and returns:\n954\t/// ```json\n955\t/// { \"success\": true }\n956\t/// ```\n957\t///\n958\t/// Rate limiting (per source IP):\n959\t/// - 10 requests per minute (configurable via `admin_ui.rate_limit.per_ip`)\n960\t/// - After 5 consecutive failed attempts, exponential backoff applies:\n961\t///   - 10m, 20m, 40m, ... up to 24h cap\n962\t///\n963\t/// Successful login resets both the rate limit counter and backoff state.\n964\tpub async fn admin_login<S>(\n965\t    State(state): State<S>,\n966\t    headers: HeaderMap,\n967\t    Json(body): Json<AdminLoginRequest>,\n968\t) -> Response\n969\twhere\n970\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n971\t    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n972\t{\n973\t    let state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n974\t\n975\t    // Extract source IP from X-Forwarded-For or X-Real-IP (trust proxy)\n976\t    let source_ip = headers\n977\t        .get(\"x-forwarded-for\")\n978\t        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n979\t        .and_then(|s| s.split(',').next())\n980\t        .or_else(|| headers.get(\"x-real-ip\").and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok()))\n981\t        .unwrap_or(\"unknown\")\n982\t        .trim()\n983\t        .to_string();\n984\t\n985\t    // Parse rate limit config\n986\t    let (limit, window_seconds) = match parse_rate_limit(&state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.per_ip) {\n987\t        Ok(parsed) => parsed,\n988\t        Err(e) => {\n989\t            error!(error = %e, \"invalid admin_ui.rate_limit.per_ip config\");\n990\t            return (\n991\t                StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,\n992\t                Json(AdminLoginResponse {\n993\t                    success: false,\n994\t                    message: Some(\"Rate limit configuration error\".into()),\n995\t                }),\n996\t            ).into_response();\n997\t        }\n998\t    };\n999\t\n1000\t    // Check rate limit and backoff\n1001\t    let backend = state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.backend.as_str();\n1002\t    if backend == \"redis\" {\n1003\t        if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n1004\t            match redis.check_rate_limit_admin_login(&source_ip, limit, window_seconds) {\n1005\t                Ok((allowed, wait_seconds)) => {\n1006\t                    if !allowed {\n1007\t                        if let Some(ws) = wait_seconds {\n1008\t                            warn!(\n1009\t                                source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n1010\t                                wait_seconds = ws,\n1011\t                                \"admin login rate limited (backoff)\"\n1012\t                            );\n1013\t                            return (\n1014\t                                StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS,\n1015\t                                Json(AdminLoginResponse {\n1016\t                                    success: false,\n1017\t                                    message: Some(format!(\n1018\t                                        \"Too many failed login attempts. Try again in {} seconds.\",\n1019\t                                        ws\n1020\t                                    )),\n1021\t                                }),\n1022\t                            ).into_response();\n1023\t                        } else {\n1024\t                            return (\n1025\t                                StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS,\n1026\t                                Json(AdminLoginResponse {\n1027\t                                    success: false,\n1028\t                                    message: Some(\"Too many login attempts. Please try again later.\".into()),\n1029\t                                }),\n1030\t                            ).into_response();\n1031\t                        }\n1032\t                    }\n1033\t                    // Allowed, proceed\n1034\t                }\n1035\t                Err(e) => {\n1036\t                    error!(error = %e, \"failed to check admin login rate limit\");\n1037\t                    // Continue anyway on error (fail-open)\n1038\t                }\n1039\t            }\n1040\t        }\n1041\t    } else if backend == \"local\" {\n1042\t        // Local backend rate limiting\n1043\t        let (allowed, wait_seconds) = state.local_rate_limiter.check(\n1044\t            &source_ip,\n1045\t            limit,\n1046\t            window_seconds * 1000,\n1047\t            state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.failed_attempt_threshold,\n1048\t            state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.backoff_start_minutes,\n1049\t            state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.backoff_max_hours * 60,\n1050\t        );\n1051\t        if !allowed {\n1052\t            warn!(\n1053\t                source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n1054\t                wait_seconds = ?wait_seconds,\n1055\t                \"admin login rate limited (local backend)\"\n1056\t            );\n1057\t            return (\n1058\t                StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS,\n1059\t                Json(AdminLoginResponse {\n1060\t                    success: false,\n1061\t                    message: if let Some(ws) = wait_seconds {\n1062\t                        Some(format!(\n1063\t                            \"Too many failed login attempts. Try again in {} seconds.\",\n1064\t                            ws\n1065\t                        ))\n1066\t                    } else {\n1067\t                        Some(\"Too many login attempts. Please try again later.\".into())\n1068\t                    },\n1069\t                }),\n1070\t            ).into_response();\n1071\t        }\n1072\t    }\n1073\t\n1074\t    // Verify admin_key (constant-time comparison to prevent timing side-channels)\n1075\t    use subtle::ConstantTimeEq as _;\n1076\t    if body.admin_key.as_bytes().ct_eq(state.config.admin.api_key.as_bytes()).into() {\n1077\t        // Successful login - reset rate limit counters\n1078\t        if backend == \"redis\" {\n1079\t            if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n1080\t                if let Err(e) = redis.reset_rate_limit_admin_login(&source_ip) {\n1081\t                    warn!(error = %e, \"failed to reset admin login rate limit\");\n1082\t                }\n1083\t            }\n1084\t        } else if backend == \"local\" {\n1085\t            state.local_rate_limiter.reset(&source_ip);\n1086\t        }\n1087\t\n1088\t        // Generate session ID and seal it\n1089\t        let session_id = generate_session_id();\n1090\t        let sealed = match seal_session(&session_id, &state.seal_key) {\n1091\t            Ok(sealed) => sealed,\n1092\t            Err(e) => {\n1093\t                error!(error = %e, \"failed to seal admin session\");\n1094\t                return (\n1095\t                    StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,\n1096\t                    Json(AdminLoginResponse {\n1097\t                        success: false,\n1098\t                        message: Some(\"Failed to create session\".into()),\n1099\t                    }),\n1100\t                ).into_response();\n1101\t            }\n1102\t        };\n1103\t\n1104\t        info!(\n1105\t            source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n1106\t            session_prefix = &session_id[..8],\n1107\t            \"admin login successful\"\n1108\t        );\n1109\t\n1110\t        // Set cookie and return success\n1111\t        (\n1112\t            StatusCode::OK,\n1113\t            [\n1114\t                (\"Set-Cookie\", format!(\"{}={}; HttpOnly; Secure; SameSite=Strict; Path=/; Max-Age={}\",\n1115\t                    COOKIE_NAME, sealed, state.config.admin_ui.session_ttl_s)),\n1116\t            ],\n1117\t            Json(AdminLoginResponse {\n1118\t                success: true,\n1119\t                message: None,\n1120\t            }),\n1121\t        ).into_response()\n1122\t    } else {\n1123\t        // Wrong admin_key - record failure for backoff tracking\n1124\t        warn!(\n1125\t            source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n1126\t            \"admin login failed: invalid admin_key\"\n1127\t        );\n1128\t\n1129\t        if backend == \"redis\" {\n1130\t            if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n1131\t                let backoff_start_minutes = state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.backoff_start_minutes;\n1132\t                let backoff_max_hours = state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.backoff_max_hours;\n1133\t                let failed_threshold = state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.failed_attempt_threshold;\n1134\t\n1135\t                if let Err(e) = redis.record_failure_admin_login(\n1136\t                    &source_ip,\n1137\t                    failed_threshold,\n1138\t                    backoff_start_minutes,\n1139\t                    backoff_max_hours,\n1140\t                ) {\n1141\t                    warn!(error = %e, \"failed to record admin login failure\");\n1142\t                }\n1143\t            }\n1144\t        } else if backend == \"local\" {\n1145\t            let backoff_start_minutes = state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.backoff_start_minutes;\n1146\t            let backoff_max_hours = state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.backoff_max_hours;\n1147\t            let failed_threshold = state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.failed_attempt_threshold;\n1148\t\n1149\t            state.local_rate_limiter.record_failure(\n1150\t                &source_ip,\n1151\t                failed_threshold,\n1152\t                backoff_start_minutes,\n1153\t                backoff_max_hours * 60,\n1154\t            );\n1155\t        }\n1156\t\n1157\t        (\n1158\t            StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED,\n1159\t            Json(AdminLoginResponse {\n1160\t                success: false,\n1161\t                message: Some(\"Invalid admin key\".into()),\n1162\t            }),\n1163\t        ).into_response()\n1164\t    }\n1165\t}\n1166\t\n1167\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n1168\t// Rebalancer Admin API Endpoints (plan §4)\n1169\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n1170\t\n1171\t/// POST /_miroir/nodes — Add a node to a replica group.\n1172\t///\n1173\t/// Request body:\n1174\t/// ```json\n1175\t/// {\n1176\t///   \"id\": \"node-new\",\n1177\t///   \"address\": \"http://node-new:7700\",\n1178\t///   \"replica_group\": 0\n1179\t/// }\n1180\t/// ```\n1181\t///\n1182\t/// Implements plan §2 \"Adding a node to an existing group\":\n1183\t/// 1. Add node to topology in `Joining` state\n1184\t/// 2. Send `NodeAdded` event to rebalancer worker\n1185\t/// 3. Worker computes affected shards and starts migration with leader lease\n1186\tpub async fn add_node<S>(\n1187\t    State(state): State<S>,\n1188\t    Json(body): Json<serde_json::Value>,\n1189\t) -> Result<Json<serde_json::Value>, (StatusCode, String)>\n1190\twhere\n1191\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n1192\t    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n1193\t{\n1194\t    let app_state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n1195\t\n1196\t    let id = body.get(\"id\")\n1197\t        .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n1198\t        .ok_or_else(|| (StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST, \"Missing 'id' field\".into()))?\n1199\t        .to_string();\n1200\t\n1201\t    let address = body.get(\"address\")\n1202\t        .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n1203\t        .ok_or_else(|| (StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST, \"Missing 'address' field\".into()))?\n1204\t        .to_string();\n1205\t\n1206\t    let replica_group = body.get(\"replica_group\")\n1207\t        .and_then(|v| v.as_u64())\n1208\t        .ok_or_else(|| (StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST, \"Missing 'replica_group' field\".into()))?\n1209\t        as u32;\n1210\t\n1211\t    // Add node to topology\n1212\t    {\n1213\t        let mut topo = app_state.topology.write().await;\n1214\t        // Check if node already exists\n1215\t        let node_id = NodeId::new(id.clone());\n1216\t        if topo.node(&node_id).is_some() {\n1217\t            return Err((StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST,\n1218\t                format!(\"Node {} already exists\", id)));\n1219\t        }\n1220\t        // Check if replica group exists\n1221\t        let group_count = topo.groups().count() as u32;\n1222\t        if replica_group >= group_count {\n1223\t            return Err((StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST,\n1224\t                format!(\"Replica group {} does not exist\", replica_group)));\n1225\t        }\n1226\t        let node = Node::new(node_id, address, replica_group);\n1227\t        topo.add_node(node);\n1228\t    }\n1229\t\n1230\t    // Send id.clone(),\n1234\t            replica_group,\n1235\t            index_uid: \"default\".to_string(),\n1236\t        };\n1237\t        if let Err(e) = worker.event_sender().try_send(event) {\n1238\t            error!(error = %e, node_id = %id, \"failed to send NodeAdded event to rebalancer worker\");\n1239\t            return Err((StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,\n1240\t                format!(\"Failed to queue rebalancing: {}\", e)));\n1241\t        }\n1242\t    }\n1243\t\n1244\t    info!(node_id = %id, replica_group, \"Node addition queued for rebalancing\");\n1245\t    Ok(Json(serde_json::json!({\n1246\t        \"node_id\": id,\n1247\t        \"replica_group\": replica_group,\n1248\t        \"message\": format!(\"Node {} added to replica group {}, rebalancing will start shortly\", id, replica_group),\n1249\t    })))\n1250\t}\n1251\t\n1252\t/// DELETE /_miroir/nodes/{id} — Remove a node from the cluster.\n1253\t///\n1254\t/// Request body (optional):\n1255\t/// ```json\n1256\t/// {\n1257\t///   \"force\": false  // Set to true to bypass draining check\n1258\t/// }\n1259\t/// ```\n1260\t///\n1261\t/// Requires the node to be in `draining` state unless `force=true`.\n1262\t/// Note: This only removes the node from topology. Draining must be completed first.\n1263\tpub async fn remove_node<S>(\n1264\t    State(state): State<S>,\n1265\t    Path(node_id): Path<String>,\n1266\t    Json(body): Json<serde_json::Value>,\n1267\t) -> Result<Json<serde_json::Value>, (StatusCode, String)>\n1268\twhere\n1269\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n1270\t    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n1271\t{\n1272\t
